English:en.### **¡Lleva tu parroquia al siguiente n:
### **Take your parish to the next digital level!**

Nowadays, having an online presence is not a luxury—it's a necessity! And don't worry, you don't have to be a tech expert to make your parish shine on the web. We take care of the technical side so you can focus on what really matters: the community.

**Why have a website for your parish?**  
A website is not just a digital business card—it's a hub of connection for your parishioners! Here are some reasons why it's indispensable:

*   **Connect with everyone**: Parishioners will always have access to the most up-to-date information, from Mass schedules to special events.
    
*   **Instant notifications**: No more forgetting! Automatic notifications let you send reminders for events, Masses, and activities directly to the community's phones.
    
*   **Accessible from anywhere**: The website works like an app on phones, and goes with you everywhere!
    

### **Your content, your style, your community**

We give you the freedom to choose what to show on your website, and we make it easy and fast. Plus, we personalize everything so your page has your parish's unique style.

### **Everything you need, hassle-free**

The goal is for you to manage everything yourself, simply. No complicated control panels or technology that makes you feel like you're programming a rocket. Our platform is easy, intuitive, and we guarantee we'll always help you with whatever you need!

So, if you want your parish to be connected, visible, and active in the digital world, we're here to help! Take the step without fear.
English:en.Necestimaos un nuevo tejado en la parroq: We need a new roof in the parish...
English:en.¿Cómo puedo solicitar el sacramento del: How can I request the sacrament of Baptism for my child?
English:en.Para solicitar el Bautismo, debes ponert: To request Baptism, you must contact the parish at least two weeks before your desired date. Parents and godparents must participate in a brief preparation, which can be done in person or online. It's a beautiful moment, so make sure to plan ahead!
English:en.¿Puedo añadir preguntas y respuestas a m: Can I add questions and answers to my website?
English:en.Sí, claro. Añadir una sección de **FAQ**:
Yes, of course. Adding a **FAQ** (Frequently Asked Questions) section to your parish website is an excellent way to make it more accessible and help parishioners with common questions. Here are some tips to do it effectively:

1.  **Organize by categories**: If you have several areas of interest (sacraments, activities, volunteering, etc.), group them so it's easier to find information.
    
2.  **Update regularly**: Make sure to review and update the answers to reflect changes or new procedures.
    
3.  **Make it accessible**: Place a visible link in the menu or on the homepage so visitors can easily access it.
    
4.  **Use a clean design**: Don't overload the page with too much information. Ideally, keep each answer brief and to the point.
English:en.Con una imagen de fondo. Y llamadas a la: With a background image. And calls to action

English:en.San Frutos y sus hermanos Valentín y Eng: Saint Frutos and his siblings, Valentín and Engracia, decided to follow God's call and gave all their money to the poor

English:en.**¿Cómo conocer el plan de Dios para tu:
**How to know God’s plan for your life?**  
Follow these recommendations and God will help you understand what He is calling you to in your life!
English:en.1. **Oración** \n La oración es una:
1.  **Prayer**  
    Prayer is a conversation with God, not just reciting prayers, but speaking with the Lord from the heart. Every day read the Bible and speak with Jesus as if He were your best friend. Ask Him to reveal His plan to you: “Jesus, I want to want what You want. Here I am, Lord; I come to do Your will.” Trust fully that God has a plan for you.
English:en.2. **Sacramentos** \n El alma es la:
2.  **Sacraments**  
    The soul is the window through which we can see God and hear His voice. If the window is dirty from sin, it must be cleaned through Confession in order to be in union with God. When you receive Communion, ask Jesus to show you your vocation.
English:en.3. **Buena información** \n Es impo:
3.  **Good information**  
    It is impossible to discover your vocation without good information. What does it mean to have a Christian and holy marriage? What do priests do? What is the life of a sister or a religious brother like? Seek reliable information and then take what you have learned back to prayer.
English:en.4. **La Santísima Virgen** \n María:
4.  **The Blessed Virgin**  
    Mary is the ultimate example of openness to God’s will. Are you open to God’s will for you? Pray three Hail Marys each day, specifically to know your vocation and to have the courage to follow it.
English:en.5. **Ir de retiro** \n ¡Los milagro:
5.  **Go on retreat**  
    Miracles happen on retreats! One of the surest ways to discover God’s plan for you is to enter into silence and prayer for an extended time. Ask trusted people to help you find a good retreat.
English:en.6. **Pide consejo** \n No trates de:
6.  **Seek counsel**  
    Do not try to discern your vocation alone, without the help of the Church. Speak with a sister, a priest, or a deacon you trust.
English:en.Cuando llegue el momento, ¡debes actuar!: When the time comes, you must act! Move in the direction your heart indicates, with the confidence that God will never send you to a place where His grace cannot sustain you.
English:en.## **Cada vocación es un misterio: cada: ## **Each vocation is a mystery: each man goes through these stages differently.**
English:en.Algunos solo pasarán por las etapas para: Some will only go through the stages to discover the meaning of their lay life and others to find their priestly vocation.
English:en.Si estás en la etapa 1 has experimentado: If you are in stage 1 you have recently experienced a call to give yourself to the Lord. This initial stage of discernment usually occurs during childhood, perhaps at Mass, for example through religious, seeing Christians who love God radically, thanks to committed catechists, with your parents, in a Catholic school class or when a priest invites you to consider the priesthood.
English:en.Esta atracción inicial y misteriosa por: This initial and mysterious attraction to give everything to God is mysterious. Becoming a priest, giving oneself to religious life, being salt and light in the midst of all people… It is even more mysterious because one has almost no information about vocation or discernment.
English:en.Las emociones principales son emoción y: The main emotions are excitement and wonder: “Could God really be calling me to give myself entirely?”
English:en.Piensas en ello de vez en cuando, pero n: You think about it from time to time, but not every day. The idea appears at Mass, when praying at night, or when someone mentions it. But you think: “Surely it’s not for me,” “I don’t need anything else,” “I already go to Mass and pray some”… “Being a layperson is not a vocation” or “I’ll get married like everyone else.”
English:en.Te das cuenta de que esa llamada no desa: You realize that this call does not go away, even if you try. You give in and stop avoiding it. You begin to pray more, to grow in faith, and to seek help from a spiritual director. Likewise, you find Christ in your brother and in those in need; you open yourself to concrete self-giving to others. You begin to fight the spiritual battle of surrendering to God’s will; some days you win the battle and other days you lose it. In this stage you discover a distinct peace, one that is born from deepening your interior life and bringing Christ into every aspect of your life. Here you learn to overcome fears: the fear of missing out, of not being enough, or of being labeled a “saint.”
English:en.Igual no encuentras todavía paz y confir: Even if you do not yet find peace and confirmation, you discover that your fears are of the priesthood or religious life: celibacy, loneliness, public speaking, being a good priest… you face them and feel the joy of serving God and others. Your desire to serve others grows and you experience happiness while serving.
English:en.Vas avanzando hacia la entrega a la volu: You are moving forward toward surrender to God’s will. After praying, informing yourself, and participating in retreats, you feel peace and you confirm God’s call to leave everything.
English:en.Tu director espiritual te dice que tiene: Your spiritual director tells you that you have the signs and qualifications. The essential element is to understand that discernment cannot advance further outside the seminary or the novitiate; you are simply going around in circles.
English:en.Entras al seminario o al noviciado y viv: You enter the seminary or the novitiate and live surrounded by others who are also discerning, and your life revolves around daily prayer, Mass, and serious study of the faith.
English:en.Habías descubierto la llamada de Dios a: You had discovered God’s call to live as a layperson and you begin to notice who might be your spouse, trusting in God. You begin to date and live courtship as discernment: prudent mutual knowledge and
English:en.Es el momento de consolidar tu camino es: It is the time to consolidate your spiritual and vocational path.
English:en.Llevas tiempo en el seminario y has crec: You have been in the seminary for some time and have grown greatly in faith, prayer, and knowledge. You know Jesus personally and you are ready to teach and serve. Now the hard work is daily, but you feel that you will most likely be a priest and you know that you can fulfill that responsibility.
English:en.Si estás a punto de casarte, también pue: If you are about to get married, you can also experience this interior peace that is born of living your vocation with self-giving. You know Jesus personally and you are ready to love, teach, and serve within your family. The daily work is constant: building a solid home, accompanying your spouse, educating the children, and living the faith together. You feel that God has called you to this mission and you know that, with His help, you can order all social life from within and transform the world through the love and self-giving of your family.
English:en.## Los votos religiosos: pobreza, castid: ## The religious vows: poverty, chastity, and obedience, a path of total love
English:en.**Por amor a Cristo, muchos hombres y mu: **For love of Christ, many men and women in the Church leave everything.** Not out of contempt for the world, but out of love for the Kingdom that is coming. In a time marked by the pursuit of pleasure, wealth, and personal autonomy, the religious vows are a **prophetic light** that shows another way of living: that of **Jesus poor, chaste, and obedient**.
English:en.Un _voto_ es una **promesa hecha a Dios*: A _vow_ is a **promise made to God**. It is not a simple human resolution, but a sacred commitment by which a person gives himself or herself to the Lord with his or her whole being. The **Code of Canon Law** defines it as a “free and deliberate promise made to God of a possible and better good” (cf. c. 1191).
English:en.Entre todos los votos, destacan los **vo: Among all vows, the **religious vows** stand out, by which the members of institutes of consecrated life solemnly commit themselves to live the _evangelical counsels_: **poverty, chastity, and obedience**. These are not mere renunciations, but **ways of loving more and better**, reproducing in one’s own life the very style of Jesus Christ.
English:en.> “Por medio de los votos de pobreza, ca:
> “Through the vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience, you can be bearers of light for the women and men of our time.”  
> — _Pope Francis, World Day for Consecrated Life_
English:en.### ✨ El voto de castidad: amar con cora: ### ✨ The vow of chastity: to love with an undivided heart
English:en.> “El consejo evangélico de castidad asu: “The evangelical counsel of chastity assumed for the sake of the Kingdom of Heaven, as a sign of the future world and a source of more abundant fruitfulness in an undivided heart, entails the obligation of perfect continence in celibacy.”
English:en.El voto de castidad no es una simple abs: The vow of chastity is not a simple abstention, but a **way of loving as Christ loved**. Whoever professes it commits to live with a heart totally given to God, directing all his or her affective capacity toward Him and toward the service of others.
English:en.En una sociedad donde el amor se mide po: In a society where love is measured by desire or utility, consecrated chastity reminds us that **true love does not possess, but gives itself**. It is the love that does not demand, but welcomes; that does not use, but **sets free**.
English:en.> “El amor casto muestra a los hombres y:
“Chaste love shows modern men and women a path to healing from isolation, in a way of loving that is free and freeing.”  
— _Pope Francis_
English:en.El consagrado vive la castidad no como r: The consecrated person lives chastity not as repression, but as an **anticipation of heaven**, where “they will no longer marry, but will be like the angels of God” (Mt 22:30). In his or her heart beats the eternal love of the divine Bridegroom, a love that **embraces everyone without being divided**.
English:en.### 💧 El voto de pobreza: libre para ama: ### 💧 The vow of poverty: free to love
English:en.> “El consejo evangélico de pobreza, en: “The evangelical counsel of poverty, in imitation of Christ who, though rich, became poor for our sake, entails, besides a life poor in fact and in spirit, a dependence and limitation in the use and disposition of goods according to the proper law of each institute.”
English:en.Vivir la pobreza evangélica no significa: Living evangelical poverty does not mean despising material goods, but **ordering them according to love**. The religious renounces owning, not because goods are evil, but because he or she wants **God to be his or her only treasure**.
English:en.En un mundo que idolatra el consumo y la: In a world that idolizes consumption and comfort, consecrated poverty reminds us that “man does not live by bread alone” (Lk 4:4). It is a silent protest against selfishness and a proclamation of interior freedom.
English:en.Ser pobre por amor es poder decir: _“Mi: To be poor for love is to be able to say: “My wealth is Christ.” It is to live detached, in solidarity, generous. It is not an emptiness, but a full availability to serve and to share.
English:en.> “La pobreza muestra cómo las cosas tie:
> “Poverty shows how things have value in the order of love, and it rejects all that can dim its beauty: selfishness, greed, dependency.”  
> — Pope Francis
English:en.### 🔥 El voto de obediencia: escuchar y: ### 🔥 The vow of obedience: listening to and following Christ
English:en.> “El consejo evangélico de obediencia,: '> “The evangelical counsel of obedience, undertaken in a spirit of faith and love in following Christ who was obedient unto death, obliges one to submit one’s will to lawful superiors when they command according to the constitutions of their own institute.”'
English:en.La obediencia es quizás el voto más exig: Obedience is perhaps the most demanding vow, because it touches the deepest part of the human being: his freedom. But it is also the one that most transforms the soul into Christ.
English:en.Jesús mismo fue “obediente hasta la muer: Jesus himself was “obedient unto death, even death on a cross” (Phil 2:8). The consecrated person learns from Him to trust, to listen, to renounce himself out of love. To obey is not to submit blindly, but to recognize the voice of God in the mediation of the Church and of superiors.
English:en.> “La obediencia es signo para nuestra s:
> “Obedience is a sign for our society because it is based on listening to one another and then acting, even at the cost of giving up one’s own tastes and preferences.”  
> — Pope Francis
English:en.En una cultura que exalta la autonomía y: In a culture that exalts autonomy and “I do what I want,” the vow of obedience proclaims a forgotten truth: true freedom is to do the will of God.
English:en.Los votos no se viven en soledad. Cada c: The vows are not lived in solitude. Every consecrated man and woman belongs to a community, where faith, prayer, and mission are shared. It is a space where one learns to love in concrete ways: in fraternal life, patience, forgiveness, service.
English:en.El religioso no busca sólo su santificac: The religious does not seek only his own sanctification, but builds together with others a spiritual family. The community thus becomes the visible face of the Kingdom of God, where all are brothers and sisters in Christ.
English:en.En un mundo sediento de autenticidad, lo: In a world thirsty for authenticity, religious men and women are living signs of hope, witnesses that it is worth giving everything for love.
English:en.**Seguir a Cristo pobre, casto y obedien: To follow Christ poor, chaste, and obedient does not impoverish life… it fills it with meaning.
English:en.¿Algunos de estos pensamientos son los t: Are some of these thoughts yours regarding celibacy?
English:en.* “¡Realmente me gustan las chicas!”\n:
* “I really like girls!”
* “I haven’t always lived a chaste life. Can I still be considered for the seminary?”
* “I struggle with purity, so I’m not sure I have the holiness necessary to commit to celibacy.”
* “Celibacy seems like a life of loneliness… will I be happy being alone?”
* “I think I still want to be a father.”
English:en.El requisito del celibato es, sin duda,: The requirement of celibacy is undoubtedly one of the greatest sources of anxiety and fear for a man discerning the priesthood. This is especially true in a sex-saturated society and culture.
English:en.El mensaje que recibimos de los medios y: The message we receive from the media and culture is very clear: no person can be happy and fulfilled if they do not have a lot of sexual activity. But this is simply not true.
English:en.> “Los medios proclaman el mensaje de qu: '> “The media proclaim the message that sex brings happiness. If this were true, we would be living in an earthly paradise and the world would be a ‘happy valley.’”'
English:en.La buena noticia es que la gracia de Dio: The good news is that God’s grace can accomplish everything. By the power of the cross of Jesus, a man can overcome sexual lust and live in peace within his vocation. It can be done. It is possible. There is much evidence.
English:en.Por ejemplo, existen aproximadamente **c: For example, there are approximately four hundred thousand Catholic priests worldwide. The great majority of them, at some point, thought or said things like those mentioned above. Well then, those four hundred thousand are now priests.
English:en.**Dios nunca nos enviará a un lugar dond: God will never send us to a place where his grace cannot sustain us.
English:en.Aunque hoy luches con la castidad, Dios: Even if you struggle with chastity today, God offers you the grace to become sexually pure. Do not let a concern about celibacy keep you from considering the priesthood.
English:en.Sobre todo, es importante comprender que: Above all, it is important to understand that priestly celibacy is not about repressing sexuality, but about proclaiming that humanity’s greatest joys are not found in earthly goods, but in union with God, in this life and in the eternal one.
English:en.> “Y todo aquel que haya dejado casas, h: '> “And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or lands for my name’s sake, will receive a hundredfold and will inherit eternal life.” (Matthew 19:29)'
English:en.> “¿Estás libre de esposa? No busques ca: '> “Are you free from a wife? Do not seek marriage... Those who marry will have worldly troubles, and I would spare you that... The unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord, how to please the Lord. But the married man is anxious about worldly things, how to please his wife, and he is divided.” (1 Corinthians 7:27–34)'
English:en.> “No todos pueden aceptar esta palabra,: '> “Not all can accept this word, but only those to whom it has been given. Some cannot marry because they were born so; others, because others made them so; and others, because they renounced marriage for the sake of the Kingdom of Heaven. Let anyone accept this who can accept it.” (Matthew 19:11–12)'
English:en.El énfasis está en el carácter especial: The emphasis is on the special character of celibacy: not all men are suited for it, but it is a vocation that gives glory to the “Kingdom of God.”
English:en.**¿Se permite salir con alguien mientras: Is it permitted to date while in the seminary? The seminary is like the engagement period for a couple: one does not date others if one wishes to build a true relationship with the chosen person. Likewise, to truly prepare and discern the call to the priesthood in the seminary, one should not date.
English:en.**¿Y si he sido sexualmente activo en el: What if I have been sexually active in the past? Does that mean I cannot be a priest? No. But now you must be in the process of embracing chastity. Saint Augustine led a very disordered life in his youth, but he abandoned his impure ways when he decided to live fully as a Catholic. There must be a significant period of “sexual sobriety” before entering the seminary, normally at least two years.
English:en.**Tengo miedo de no poder mantenerme cas: I am afraid I will not be able to remain chaste. If you ask God, He will give you abundant graces to live a chaste life. You must respond to those graces by making chaste decisions. As you grow in chastity and it becomes a habit of your life, you will experience strength and ease in living it. Celibacy is a serious commitment that must be at the center of discernment; it is not for everyone, but it is possible for those who are called.
English:en.**Me parece antinatural vivir toda la vi: It seems unnatural to me to live a whole life without sex. Most men and women are naturally called to marriage. That is why living a chaste and celibate life goes beyond the ordinary; it is, in fact, a supernatural call from God to live a radical love for the Church. Yes, it is a sacrifice, but the rewards are great. Throughout the centuries, many saints, religious, monks, nuns, and priests have lived very full and happy lives.
English:en.> “Soy muy impaciente con algunos de los: '> “I am very impatient with some of the pragmatic arguments in favor of celibacy—that it frees up time and allows one to focus on other things. I prefer to see celibacy as an irrational, overflowing, poetic and symbolic expression of the soul in love.” — Fr. Robert Barron'
English:en.Los sacerdotes del Rito Latino renuncian: Priests of the Latin Rite renounce their natural right to marriage “for the sake of the Kingdom of Heaven,” as Jesus taught his disciples (Mt 19:12). It is a gift from God that opens a man’s heart to embrace all of God’s children in a profound way. His healthy and holy inclination toward marriage and family is transformed into a supernatural fatherhood, which makes his ministry—if he is faithful—extraordinarily fruitful.
English:en.### **Otras realidades espirituales que: ### Other spiritual realities that celibacy signifies
English:en.* El celibato marca al sacerdote como:
* Celibacy marks the priest as a man consecrated to the service of Christ and the Church. It shows, in a concrete way, that he is not simply someone who performs functions or holds an office, but that he has been transformed in his being by the sacrament of Holy Orders.
* Celibacy configures the priest more closely to Christ, the great High Priest, who renounced earthly marriage for the Kingdom and to be more perfectly united to his heavenly Bride, the Church.
* It is fitting that the priest who offers this same Jesus in sacrifice to the Father should manifest in his own person (though imperfectly) the purity and holiness of the immaculate Victim.
* Celibacy reminds us of heaven, pointing to the coming of the Kingdom, when marriage will no longer exist.
Collaborate, the seminary belongs to everyone.
English:en.## **Acompañar, rezar y sostener las voc: ## Accompany, pray for, and support vocations
English:en.La Iglesia, a lo largo de los siglos, nu: The Church, throughout the centuries, has never lacked vocations to the ministerial priesthood. Some periods were more fruitful and others less. But today the question arises: Is the ministerial priesthood really necessary? The answer is clear: Yes!
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\">Se neces: Priests are needed so that the message of Christ and His presence in the Sacraments may continue shaping the history of salvation in humanity and in the People of God of all ages.
English:en.No podemos medir las vocaciones por la c: We cannot measure vocations by quantity compared with past decades. Complaints are useless. What matters is to **look with the eyes of Christ**, go out to evangelize and go on mission even to small communities. Perhaps the time has come to **restore small communities**, to be **leaven**, even if it is a small leaven. As Jesus reminds us:
English:en.“El Reino de los Cielos es como la levad: “The Kingdom of Heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed with three measures of flour, until it was all leavened” (Mt 13:33)
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\">Cada cri: Each Christian, living in the world without losing his nature, draws people to the Lord by his example and his word.
English:en.Muchas veces los movimientos sociales y: Many times social movements and cultural trends do not facilitate a Gospel way of life, but that **must not prevent us from showing that the Christian life is the best inheritance we can offer**. Example and witness make an impression on those who observe us. Saint Augustine lived it this way:
English:en.> Si isti et istae, cur non ego?” (Si es: > Si isti et istae, cur non ego?” (If these men and women have done it, why not me?)
English:en.Fue el testimonio de sus compañeros lo q: It was the testimony of his companions that made him reflect, abandon a dissolute and superficial life, and joyfully embrace the Gospel life, becoming a priest and, in time, a Bishop.
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\">Hoy ocur: Today the same happens: the witness of life attracts vocations. That is why we must encourage families, consecrated persons, priests, and all the faithful of the People of God to live the following of Jesus radically.
English:en.Muchos seminaristas nos cuentan su exper: Many seminarians tell us about their experience:
English:en.* “Me encontré con un sacerdote que me: *   “I met a priest whose life fascinated me, and I considered living like him.”
English:en.* “En mi familia rezábamos juntos todo: *   “In my family we prayed together every day, and reading the Gospel I felt the call to be a priest.”
English:en.* “Vi a religiosas atendiendo a los má: *   “I saw religious sisters caring for those most in need and I wanted to be like them.”
English:en.* “Un día sentí un vacío después de un: *   “One day I felt an emptiness after a party, I entered a church and experienced a deep joy. Now I am a cloistered nun.”
English:en.* “La vida en comunidad despertó mi en: *   “Life in community awakened my dedication to the priesthood.”
English:en.Cuando se vive **desde las fuentes del A: When one lives **from the sources of God’s Love**, the vocational call **attracts like a magnet**.
English:en.La vocación nace cuando **se escucha a D: Vocation is born when **one listens to God in interior silence and in daily prayer**. Therefore, the **first step** is
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\"><strong>: to pray for seminarians, even naming specific persons: a young man in your family, a friend, a parish companion… Make your prayer personal and persistent.
English:en.Luego, **invita a los jóvenes a atrevers: Then, **invite young people to dare to consider their vocation**. Show them the beauty of following Christ, share stories of generosity and self-giving, and accompany them with example and prayer.
English:en.Pero la colaboración no termina en la or: But collaboration does not end in prayer. We can also **support their formation financially**.
English:en.Hoy es fácil y seguro hacerlo mediante: Today it is easy and safe to do so through
English:en.* Mediante transferencia a la cuenta:: *   By bank transfer to the account: **ES00 0000 0000 0000 0000 00** _(replace this number with the real one)_.
English:en.Cada aporte, grande o pequeño, ayuda a *: Each contribution, large or small, helps **form future priests**, ensure their well-being, and sustain their mission of service to all of us. With your prayer and your collaboration, **we help the seed of vocation to grow and bear abundant fruit**. God will reward us for every effort and every generous gesture!
English:en.Cómo elegir bien: amar con los ojos de D: How to choose well: to love with God’s eyes
English:en.**Elegir con quién compartir la vida** e:
**Choosing with whom to share life** is one of the most important decisions a Christian can make.  
It is not only about **finding someone you like**, but about **discovering the person whom God is calling you to love and serve for life**.
English:en.**Amar con los ojos de Dios** significa: **To love with God’s eyes** means to look beyond emotion and impulse, and **to learn to decide with the heart and the mind enlightened by faith.**
English:en.### **No te dejes cegar por los sentimie: ### **Do not let yourself be blinded by feelings**
English:en.El amor verdadero no es un _tsunami_ de:
True love is not a tsunami of emotions that sweeps you along without thinking.  
In the first phases — attraction and infatuation — there is a strong emotional and hormonal load that can cloud reason. It is normal, but you must let the wave subside to be able to see clearly.
English:en.> **“Cuando te preguntas ‘¿me conviene?’:
> **“When you ask yourself ‘Is this good for me?’, you are on the right track.”**  
> Not because love is calculation, but because mature love knows how to discern.
English:en.Enamorarse no basta: **hay que decidir a:
Falling in love is not enough: **you have to decide to love.**  
Or, as we often say: _you fall in love with the heart, but you choose with the head._
English:en.Hoy parece que las relaciones funcionan:
Today it seems that relationships work like in series: two people meet, have dinner, like each other… and everything rushes ahead.  
But love **does not grow by bursts of impulse.**
English:en.La **sexualidad** está pensada como un *:
**Sexuality** is intended as a **language of conjugal love**, and when it is brought forward, it **confuses.**  
The body promises what the soul has not yet decided to give.
English:en.> **“El sexo puede nublar el discernimie: > **“Sex can cloud discernment: it makes you think there is unity when it has not yet been built.”**
English:en.Muchos noviazgos se rompen porque **nunc:
Many courtships break up because **they never truly knew each other**, because they spoke little and touched much.  
That is why the advice remains valid and wise:  
**talk a lot, pray together, and touch little.**
English:en.El noviazgo **no es un “mini matrimonio”:
Courtship **is not a “mini marriage”**: it is a time to **discern whether this is the person with whom you want to share life.**  
It is not only about having a good time, but about **discovering whether together you can grow in holiness.**
English:en.El **noviazgo cristiano** es una **escue:
**Christian courtship** is a **school of love**.  
It is not about possessing, but about **learning to give yourself, to respect, to understand.**
English:en.En él, uno aprende a pasar del **enamora: In it, one learns to move from **infatuation — which is feeling — to love — which is deciding —.**
English:en.Es el momento de conocerse a fondo:: It is the time to get to know each other in depth:
English:en.* Cómo reaccionáis ante el dolor o la: *   How you react to pain or frustration
English:en.> **No basta con que la otra persona sea: > **It is not enough that the other person is good: you have to see how you are together.**
English:en.* ¿Cómo actuamos cuando hay desacuerdo: *   How do we act when there are disagreements?
English:en.* ¿Sabemos pedir perdón y ceder?: *   Do we know how to ask for forgiveness and give way?
English:en.* ¿Compartimos una misma fe y proyecto: *   Do we share the same faith and life project?
English:en.* ¿Nos ayudamos a ser mejores o nos de: *   Do we help each other become better, or do we wear each other down?
English:en.* ¿Nos amamos con libertad, sin depend: *   Do we love each other with freedom, without emotional dependence?
English:en.El amor cristiano **no busca la perfecci: Christian love **does not seek the perfection of the other**, but **to walk together toward it.**
English:en.En el discernimiento de pareja hay que m:
In discernment as a couple, we must look beyond what is superficial.  
**A home is not built on beauty or charm, but on the soul.**  
Appearance passes; the heart and faith remain.
First, that he be **truly Christian.**  
Not just baptized, but **a believer, practicing, someone who lives his faith** and desires to walk with you toward God.
English:en.> Muchos matrimonios sufren porque se un:
> Many marriages suffer because they united without sharing the same faith, hoping the other would “change.”  
> **Love does not convert by itself: only grace and freedom do.**
English:en.También mira la **madurez**: cómo vive s: Also look at **maturity**: how he lives with his family, how he works, how he faces difficulties, how he manages money, whether he is responsible, faithful, and honest.
English:en.No te fíes de los que **prometen mucho y:
Do not trust those who **promise much and live little.**  
Habits do not change by magic in marriage.
English:en.> **“Antes de viajar por tierra, ora; si:
> **“Before you travel by land, pray; if by sea, pray twice; and if you are going to marry, pray three times.”**  
> — _Russian proverb_
English:en.El matrimonio no se construye sobre una: Marriage is not built on a smile or on a moment of tenderness, but on all that you are: **thoughts, dreams, wounds, hopes, and faith.**
English:en.Casarse no es simplemente “quererse much:
Getting married is not simply “loving each other very much.”  
It is **to want to be spouses**, to form a **community of life open to children and to God.**
English:en.Por eso, el noviazgo debe ser un **camin: That is why courtship should be a **path of preparation**, a process in which it is decided—with freedom and truth—whether that love can become a **sacrament.**
English:en.No te fíes de los flechazos ni de las pr:
Do not trust love at first sight or haste.  
Nor appearance or attractiveness.  
Seek a **solid** love, founded on faith, virtue, and trust.
English:en.**Elige con oración.** \nEscucha a quie:
**Choose with prayer.**  
Listen to those who love you: parents, priests, friends in the faith.  
Seek counsel, but above all **ask God for light.**  
He knows your heart better than anyone.
English:en.**Amar con los ojos de Dios** es mirar a:
**To love with God’s eyes** is to look at the other as He looks: with **truth, tenderness, and challenge.**  
It is **to desire the good of the other more than one’s own pleasure.**  
It is **to decide to love even when it is not felt**, and to trust that the feeling will return, purified and stronger.
English:en.> **“El amor humano es serio, grande y s: > **“Human love is serious, great, and holy.”**
English:en.Por eso hay que construirlo sobre cimien:
Therefore it must be built on solid foundations:  
**prayer, chastity, sincerity, faith, and the will to serve.**
English:en.**Elegir bien no es tener suerte:** \ne:
**Choosing well is not about luck:**  
it is **knowing how to look with a heart in grace.**
English:en.Para llegar a ser sacerdote católico, un: To become a Catholic priest, a man must fulfill three basic requirements:
English:en.En una palabra: **intensa**, pero tambié:
In a word: **intense**, but also **full of life**.  
The demands of the priesthood are great, which is why the formation is rigorous… but in the midst of study and prayer there is **time to live together, laugh, and share as brothers**.
English:en.Además de los estudios a nivel de maestr: In addition to master’s-level studies, seminarians:
English:en.* Rezán juntos al menos dos veces al d: *   Pray together at least twice a day.
English:en.* Realizan **prácticas pastorales** en: *   Carry out **pastoral practices** in local parishes.
English:en.Y entre todo eso, hay momentos para **el:
And among all that, there are moments for **sports, get-togethers, jokes in the dining hall, and long talks among friends** that forge an authentic fraternity.  
Because the seminary not only forms priests: **it forms brothers who learn to live in community, with joy, and with Christ at the center**.
English:en.Ser sacerdote **no es un trabajo**, sino:
Being a priest **is not a job**, but **a new identity**: to become an _alter Christus_, another Christ.  
Therefore, the Church proposes a demanding formation in **four fundamental areas**:
English:en.1. **Formación humana:** cultivar una p: 1.  **Human formation:** to cultivate a personality that is a **bridge to Christ** and that allows one to be an effective spokesperson of the Church.
English:en.2. **Formación espiritual:** desarrolla: 2.  **Spiritual formation:** to develop a **deep and mature relationship with Christ** through prayer and a virtuous life.
English:en.3. **Formación intelectual:** conocer a: 3.  **Intellectual formation:** to know in depth the **truths of the faith** and acquire the skills to teach them to others.
English:en.4. **Formación pastoral:** aprender a s: 4.  **Pastoral formation:** to learn to be a true **shepherd of souls**, accompanying the faithful in their joys and sufferings.
English:en.El camino hacia el sacerdocio es una ver:
The path to the priesthood is a true adventure of grace: a **journey where the heart learns to listen, to serve, and to love like Christ, the Good Shepherd**.  
In our seminary, each stage of formation has its own rhythm and beauty, helping each young man grow in freedom, maturity, and joyful self-giving.
English:en.Es el **primer año**, un tiempo para **c:
It is the **first year**, a time to **clarify and strengthen the priestly vocation**.  
It is a year of **prayer, community life, and fellowship**, where each seminarian learns to leave behind his own ideas about vocation and open his heart to the **voice of God**.  
It focuses on **creating a life of prayer and fraternity** that will sustain the entire formative path. The year is lived in Alcalá de Henares, together with seminarians from Madrid and Alcalá, reinforcing discernment and fraternity.
English:en.En esta etapa contamos con **Nico y Alej: In this stage we have **Nico and Alejandro**, taking their first steps in faith and formation.
English:en.Normalmente dura **dos años** y tiene co:
It normally lasts **two years** and aims to **verify and strengthen the vocational choice** through discipleship.  
Here the seminarian **lives the interior life by following Jesus**, grows in virtues, dedicates himself to the **study of Philosophy** and actively participates in **social-charitable activities**.
English:en.En esta etapa tenemos a **Álvaro y Carlo: In this stage we have **Álvaro and Carlos**, deepening in spiritual and pastoral life.
English:en.Normalmente dura **tres años** y se dedi:
It normally lasts **three years** and is devoted to the **assimilation and identification with the being and ministry of the diocesan presbyter**.  
The seminarian is configured to Christ, the Good Shepherd, integrating **all aspects of his personality into the choice for the presbyterate**.  
He deepens in prayer, relationship with God, the **study of Theology**, and on weekends devotes himself to **pastoral work in the parish** where he is assigned.
English:en.En esta etapa contamos con **Andrés,** e: In this stage we have **Andrés,** in his third year of seminary:
English:en.Y **Andoni y Miguel**, en sexto curso, c: And **Andoni and Miguel**, in sixth year, growing in experience and pastoral dedication.
English:en.Es **el año de inserción pastoral** y de:
It is **the year of pastoral insertion** and of definitive preparation for the presbyterate.  
It is the **synthesis of the formative journey**, where the **orders of the diaconate** are normally received and, at the end, **the priesthood**.  
It is about **living the total gift of life and service to all**, consolidating everything learned in the previous stages.
English:en.Aquí tenemos a **David, Ion y Manu**, vi: Here we have **David, Ion, and Manu**, living the mission of serving with a pastor’s heart.
English:en.Si sientes que Dios te llama o simplemen: If you feel that God is calling you or you simply want to **talk about your vocation**, the **Vocations Ministry of Navarre** is here to accompany you.
English:en.* **Iñigo Beunza Sola**, **sacerdote y: *   **Iñigo Beunza Sola**, **priest and in charge of Vocations Ministry**. 660 36 39 53
English:en.* **Perdon**, **virgen consagrada y co: *   **Perdon**, **consecrated virgin and collaborator in Vocations Ministry**. 680 58 13 89
English:en.Ellos estarán **encantados de escucharte: They will be **delighted to listen to you, guide you, and walk with you** on this path of discovery.
English:en.### No se discierne solo: acompáñate en: ### One does not discern alone: accompany yourself in your vocation
English:en.Discernir la llamada de Dios no es un ca: Discerning God’s call is not a path traveled in solitude. In our diocese, there are **discernment groups** that meet regularly at the Seminary to accompany those who feel called to the priesthood. There one reflects, prays, and receives guidance from those who already live this vocation up close.
English:en.Además, hay **grupos más amplios** que s: In addition, there are **broader groups** that meet periodically to receive formation and grow in the understanding of Christian life and vocation in general. These meetings help to clarify one’s own vocation and to take concrete steps toward it.
English:en.Si quieres informarte o unirte a alguno: If you want to get information or join one of these groups, contact your parish priest! Or talk to a priest you trust. If not, you can contact:
English:en.**Iñigo Beunza** – Delegado de Pastoral:
**Iñigo Beunza** – Delegate for Vocations Ministry and Seminary Formator  
**Phone:** 660 36 39 53
English:en.Jovenes que se preguntan para quienes so: Young people who ask themselves for whom they are
English:en.Claves del discernimiento y formación vo: Keys to discernment and vocational formation
English:en.Chicos jovenes que disciernen su vocació: Young men who discern their priestly vocation
English:en.A pesar de esta vocación universal, **mu: Despite this universal vocation, **many baptized people have not yet become fully aware of their belonging to the Church**. Many consider themselves Catholic, but they do not experience the Church as **a home that transforms their life and their environment**. This generates situations where Christian values are seen as something “optional” or cultural, instead of being **the foundation of their identity**.
English:en.* La vida profesional, política, econó: *   Professional, political, economic, scientific, artistic, and media life **is not always guided by evangelical criteria**.
English:en.* Existe una **incoherencia entre la f: *   There exists an **incoherence between the professed faith and real commitment** in everyday life.
English:en.* Los laicos no siempre reciben **acom: *   The laity do not always receive **adequate accompaniment from pastors**, which makes it difficult to discover and mature their vocation.
English:en.Algunos desafíos vienen de **actitudes i: Some challenges come from **attitudes within the Church**:
English:en.* Persistencia de una **mentalidad cle: *   Persistence of a **clerical mentality**, which limits the initiative of the laity in evangelizing action.
English:en.* Dedicación preferente de muchos laic: *   Preferential dedication of many laypeople to **intra-ecclesial tasks**, without projecting their faith toward society.
English:en.* Deficiencias en la **formación integ: *   Deficiencies in **integral formation**, which prevent an effective response to the current challenges of society.
English:en.Estos factores hacen necesario un **rede: These factors make necessary a **profound rediscovery of the lay vocation**. Being Christian is not only fulfilling religious practices: **it is to have a vocation**, and being a lay person means **ordering the whole of social life from within**, from the heart of current reality, with the light of the Gospel.
English:en.El **Concilio Vaticano II** restableció: The **Second Vatican Council** restored the **permanent diaconate** (LG 29) and defined it as “a distinct and permanent degree within the hierarchy.” It may be conferred on **married men** who receive the **sacrament of Holy Orders** in its **third degree**.
English:en.Los diáconos son **fortalecidos por la i: Deacons are **strengthened by the laying on of hands** that comes from the Apostles and are more closely united to the **service of the altar**, carrying out their ministry with the **grace of the diaconate** (AG 16).
English:en.El diácono está llamado a vivir la _diak: The deacon is called to live the diakonia—the **service**—that characterizes his vocation. His mission is threefold: **to serve the Word, the Liturgy, and Charity**.
English:en.* Administrar los **sacramentos del Ba:
*   Administer the **sacraments of Baptism and Matrimony**.
*   **Assist the priest** at the Eucharist, **proclaim the Gospel**, and **distribute Communion**, both in the church and to the sick.
*   **Preach**, **give catechesis**, and **preside at funerals**.
*   **Administer parishes** and coordinate works of charity.
English:en.El diácono no sustituye al sacerdote ni: The deacon does not replace the priest or the lay faithful, but has **his own identity and mission**, within the one service to the People of God.
English:en.El diaconado se apoya en el **Nuevo Test: The diaconate is rooted in the **New Testament** and expresses Christ’s mandate regarding service:
English:en.> “No he venido a que me sirvan, sino a: > “I did not come to be served, but to serve” (cf. Lk 22:27).
English:en.Jesús se presenta como el **primer diáco: Jesus presents himself as the **first deacon**, the servant par excellence.
English:en.En los **Hechos de los Apóstoles**, los: In the **Acts of the Apostles**, the Twelve choose **seven reputable men**, filled with the Holy Spirit, to serve at table and assist those in need (Acts 6:1–6). Among them, **Stephen**, the first martyr, and **Philip**, called “the evangelist” (Acts 21:8), stand out.
English:en.Desde los primeros siglos, el diaconado: From the earliest centuries, the diaconate closely accompanied the bishop and had great importance in ecclesial life. The deacon **Saint Lawrence**, patron and example, was an administrator and almoner of the Church of Rome, a model of dedication and fidelity.
English:en.El **Concilio Vaticano II** recuperó el: The **Second Vatican Council** recovered the diaconate as a stable ministry, with **its own identity and mission**, opening a new stage in the life of the Church.
English:en.El diaconado permanente sigue creciendo: The permanent diaconate continues to grow as a **response of the Holy Spirit** to the Church’s current needs. In our diocese, **three men** have received this ministry: **two married and one celibate**. Each one, from his state in life, lives the deacon’s motto:
English:en.Dificultades y rupturas: cuando el amor: Difficulties and breakups: when love also purifies
English:en.### Dificultades y rupturas: cuando el a: ### Difficulties and breakups: when love also purifies
English:en.**Amar no siempre es fácil.** \nTodo am:
**Loving is not always easy.**  
Every true love goes through moments of trial, doubts, wounds, or even breakups. But even there—in what hurts—there can be a call from God. Love, when it is authentic, not only makes one happy: it also purifies, teaches, and transforms.
English:en.En el noviazgo surgen diferencias, decep:
In courtship, differences, disappointments, weariness, or fear of the future arise. Sometimes one feels they are not reciprocated as they expected, or that the relationship has cooled. This is normal.  
Love is not a straight path, but a school where God teaches us to **love beyond ourselves**.
English:en.> “El amor no consiste en mirarse el uno:
> “Love does not consist in gazing at each other, but in looking outward together in the same direction.”  
> —Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
English:en.Las dificultades pueden ser un espejo do: Difficulties can be a mirror where we discover our weaknesses, our selfishness, or our wounds. Instead of running from them, it is an opportunity to grow, to learn to forgive, and to trust God more.
English:en.### Cuando toca soltar: ### When it’s time to let go
English:en.A veces, el amor también se purifica en:
Sometimes, love is also purified in **renunciation**. There are relationships that are not called to continue, and discerning this with sincerity and prayer is also an act of love.  
Breaking up does not always mean failing: it can be the most honest and courageous way to love. God does not bless just any relationship, but the one that leads us to holiness and true self-gift.
English:en.**Soltar con paz** es reconocer que el o: **Letting go with peace** is recognizing that the other belongs to God before they belong to us, and that happiness is not measured by keeping, but by loving in freedom.
English:en.> “Amar es querer el bien del otro, aunq: > “To love is to will the good of the other, even if that good does not include me.”
English:en.Después de una ruptura, pueden quedar he:
After a breakup, there may remain wounds, feelings of guilt, or emptiness. But the heart is not made to remain broken: **God can heal** what seemed lost.  
It is a time of silence, prayer, and accompaniment. Of letting grace repair what human love could not achieve.
English:en.Los sacramentos, la dirección espiritual: The sacraments, spiritual direction, and friendship are medicine. The Eucharist sustains, confession frees, prayer consoles. It is not only about “turning the page,” but about **letting God write a new one**.
English:en.Las pruebas del amor no son un obstáculo: The trials of love are not an obstacle, but a purification. Mature love is born from pain embraced with faith. When it gives itself, forgives, and learns, the heart expands and loves better.
English:en.Por eso, incluso las rupturas pueden ser: Therefore, even breakups can be part of the vocational journey: they help one to know oneself, to purify desires, and to be disposed to love like Christ, with truth and without fear.
English:en.* No tengas miedo de pedir ayuda: habl: *   Do not be afraid to ask for help: speaking with a priest or a spiritual companion can clarify much.
English:en.* No idealices ni te encierres: el nov: *   Do not idealize or close yourself off: Christian courtship is a path of freedom, not of dependence.
English:en.* No apagues la esperanza: si Dios ha: *   Do not quench hope: if God has permitted a wound, it is because he wants to bring forth from it a greater love.
English:en.La vocación es el compromiso profundo de: Vocation is the deep commitment to follow God’s call. Effort and care are required to truly understand that call. This process of discovering God’s will is known as **vocational discernment**.
English:en.El discernimiento adecuado implica vario: Proper discernment involves several key elements:
English:en.* **Profundo autoconocimiento:** compr: *   **Deep self-knowledge:** understanding your personality, strengths and weaknesses, inclinations, spirituality, mental capacities, and even your physical health.
English:en.* **Un conocimiento sólido y un amor s: *   **A solid knowledge of and a sincere love for our Catholic faith.**
English:en.* **Familiaridad con las diferentes vo: *   **Familiarity with the different vocations**, what they entail, and the way of life they shape in those who embrace them.
English:en.Naturalmente, este proceso no sucede de: Naturally, this process does not happen overnight. Vocational discernment is a **journey** that can take months or even years. It requires study, prayer, and, above all, patience.
English:en.* El discernimiento **no es algo que s: *   Discernment **is not something that can be done in isolation**. The best way to discern is with the guidance of the Church, ideally accompanied by a priest or a trusted spiritual director who helps you discover where God may be calling you.
English:en.* Si buscas orientación —ya sea que te: *   If you are seeking guidance—whether you have simple questions or need deeper accompaniment—**we are here to help!**
English:en.El noviazgo como vocación y camino de en: Courtship as a vocation and path of self-giving
English:en.### El noviazgo como vocación y camino d: ### Courtship as a vocation and path of self-giving
English:en.**El noviazgo no es un paréntesis en la:
**Courtship is not a parenthesis in the Christian life, but a true call to love like Christ.**  
Just as marriage is a vocation to unconditional self-giving, courtship is a time of discernment and maturation in love. It is the stage where two people get to know each other, discover each other, and prepare to decide whether they want to give themselves to each other forever.
English:en.### Un tiempo de discernimiento y gracia: ### A time of discernment and grace
English:en.Cada cristiano está llamado a la santida: Every Christian is called to holiness —including those who are dating—. This time is not a pause, but an opportunity to grow in faith and virtue. Living courtship with God at the center means letting Him give meaning to the relationship: illuminating affections, purifying intentions, and making love fruitful.
English:en.Cuando un amor te acerca más a Dios y te: When a love brings you closer to God and helps you love others better, there is a Christian courtship. The more the couple love each other, the more capable they are of loving God, and vice versa.
> “The heart has been created to love. Let us therefore bring Christ into all our loves.”
English:en.Amar no es solo sentir, sino aprender a: To love is not only to feel, but to learn to give oneself. Courtship is a school of love where virtues are forged: respect, patience, generosity, and fidelity. Small gestures —listening, yielding, waiting— prepare for a love that lasts.
English:en.También es un tiempo para **formarse y c: It is also a time to **be formed and grow together**. Reading, dialoguing, sharing concerns about faith and marriage helps to build on rock. It is important to allow oneself to be accompanied, to seek counsel, and to learn from those who are already walking that path.
English:en.Un amor auténtico cuida y respeta. La ca: Authentic love cares and respects. Chastity is not a denial of love, but its protection. It teaches how to love without using, to wait in order to give oneself completely, to discover the beauty of the beloved person as a gift. Christian courtship is not measured by the intensity of desire, but by the depth of respect and the truth of commitment.
English:en.> Amar es ayudar al otro a ser santo, no: > To love is to help the other to be holy, not to be an obstacle on their way to God.
English:en.El noviazgo también es tiempo de **amist: Courtship is also a time of **friendship, laughter, and shared life**. It is not about closing in on the relationship, but about opening yourselves together to the world: serving, doing apostolate, living the faith with joy. A Christian couple do not only love each other: they are **a witness to the love of God in the midst of the world.**
English:en.Los novios que rezan juntos, que van a M: Couples who pray together, who go to Mass, who go to confession, who put God at the center, experience that their love becomes stronger and freer. In the sacraments one finds the grace to forgive each other, to understand each other, and to grow in communion.
English:en.El noviazgo es, en definitiva, **una voc: Courtship is, ultimately, **a vocation to true love**, an invitation to learn to love as Christ loves His Church: with patience, with tenderness, and with self-giving.
English:en.El papel de la familia y la comunidad: The role of the family and the community
English:en.### **El primer hogar de la vocación: la: ### **The first home of the vocation: the family**
English:en.Toda vocación comienza en casa. No en un:
Every vocation begins at home. Not in a seminary, nor in a convent, but **in the warmth of a family that prays, loves, and serves**.  
There one learns to say “yes,” to look beyond oneself, to discover that life becomes great when it is given.
English:en.* La familia es el **primer seminario: *   The family is the **first seminary of the heart**.
English:en.* Donde se enseña a **hablar con Dios: *   Where one is taught to **speak with God naturally**.
English:en.* Donde se aprende que la fe **no es t: *   Where one learns that faith **is not theory, but shared life**.
English:en.### **La comunidad: cuna donde crece la: ### **The community: cradle where the call grows**
English:en.Nadie discierne solo. \nLa **comunidad:
No one discerns alone.  
The **parish community**, the group, the movement, are places where faith becomes a living experience.  
There one **discovers one’s gifts, shares concerns, and finds concrete models** of self-giving.
English:en.👉 La comunidad no sustituye la voz de Di:
👉 The community does not replace the voice of God, but **helps to recognize it**.  
👉 It accompanies, encourages, sustains… and **prays**.
English:en.> _Una comunidad que reza por las vocaci: > _A community that prays for vocations brings them to birth._
English:en.Necesitamos familias y comunidades que *:
We need families and communities that **live their own vocation with joy**, knowing that this is already a witness.  
When a married couple live in unity, when a priest serves with passion, when a religious sister smiles with faith, **they are preaching with their life**.
English:en.La vocación no es un tema del pasado ni:
Vocation is not a topic of the past nor only for “others.”  
It is the **great adventure of every Christian**: to discover how God wants to love the world through you.
English:en.🌾 **Padres, catequistas, sacerdotes, ami:
🌾 **Parents, catechists, priests, friends…**  
Let us speak about vocation naturally.  
Let us look at our young people with eyes of faith:
English:en.> “Este chico sería un buen sacerdote.”:
> “This boy would be a good priest.”  
> “That girl has a big heart for service.”
English:en.Y no solo lo pensemos: **recemos por ell:
And let us not only think it: **let us pray for them**.  
Each prayer is a seed in God’s field.
English:en.Vive tu familia como Iglesia: ama, entre: Live your family as Church: love, give, and be a beacon of God’s love in the world.
English:en.Cristo quiso nacer y crecer en la Sagrad: Christ willed to be born and to grow in the Holy Family of Joseph and Mary. From its origins, the family has been **the first nucleus of Christian life**, where faith is taught, lived, and transmitted. Every believing household is a **domestic Church**, a place where the love of God is first preached through mutual self-giving and daily example.
English:en.Los padres son los **primeros predicador:
Parents are the **first preachers of the faith**. With their words and especially with their example, they teach their children to live in God.  
They foster vocations and help each member of the family to discover their personal call. Holiness is learned at home, in patience, forgiveness, work, and daily prayer.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida:** Padres leyendo la B: **Suggested image:** Parents reading the Bible to their children, showing closeness and unity.
English:en.La familia cristiana es **una comunión d:
The Christian family is **a communion of persons**, reflecting the communion that exists in God between Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.  
Through prayer, the sacraments, and daily life, the members exercise **the baptismal priesthood**, offering their life as a sign of love and self-giving. Here one learns to be generous, patient, and to love without reserve.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida:** Familia en oración: **Suggested image:** Family praying together, in an attitude of recollection.
English:en.Como Dios crea y educa, la familia parti: As God creates and educates, the family participates in His work: **to procreate, educate, and form in love**. Each home is an **image of God**, responsible for transmitting life, love, and hope. The family’s mission begins by preaching first the love of God among its members, in daily self-giving and mutual care.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida:** Familia abrazada en: **Suggested image:** Family embracing in a natural landscape, reflecting love and creation.
English:en.Vivir la fe en familia no es solo cuidar: Living the faith in the family is not only about caring for one’s own home, it is **to be a beacon for others**. Daily acts of love and service teach the children and show those around us that the Christian life is true and beautiful. The family evangelizes by its example, its commitment, and its self-giving.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida:** Familia participand: **Suggested image:** Family participating in parish activities or community service.
Family prayer unites and strengthens. Reading the Word of God, praying together, and placing God at the center of daily life makes a difference in decisions, behaviors, and emotional bonds.  
If the home revolves only around worldly things, the sacred dimension of life is lost. A family that prays remains united and transmits the faith.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida:** Familia rezando ant: Suggested image: Family praying before a meal or in an everyday moment of unity.
English:en.La familia cristiana es **una Iglesia vi: The Christian family is a living Church present in the world, a reflection of God’s love. Each member has the dignity and responsibility to preach first with their life and self-giving to the other. The home not only lives the faith; it radiates it; it forms, accompanies, and evangelizes. Being a Christian family is a call to love without limits, to overcome selfishness, and to live total self-giving, as God loves us.
English:en.**Imagen sugerida al final:** Collage de: Suggested image at the end: Collage of Christian families in prayer, celebrations, and service, showing unity and diversity.
English:en.1. ¿Qué hace un sacerdote? \n El pr:
1.  What does a priest do?  
    A priest’s purpose is to bring people to Jesus and Jesus to people. He does this primarily by preaching the Word and offering the Sacrifice of the Mass.
English:en.Su vida diaria consiste en administrar l: His daily life consists of administering the sacraments—Baptism, Confirmation, Eucharist, Penance and Reconciliation, Anointing of the Sick, and Marriage (Holy Orders is administered by the bishop)—and of attending to the spiritual and human needs of his community.
English:en.2. ¿Qué es exactamente una “vocación”?:
2.  What exactly is a “vocation”?  
    A vocation is a call from God to do something specific for Him and for His Kingdom. The primary vocation of every person is to be holy: to love and serve God, to obey His commandments, and to cooperate with Christ in the work of redemption by loving and serving others.
English:en.Pero todos somos llamados también a una: But we are all also called to a secondary vocation, a “state of life” in which we are to be holy.
English:en.3. ¿Cuáles son esas “vocaciones secunda:
3.  What are those “secondary vocations” to which I might be called?  
    Many people are called to marriage, but it is a mistake to assume automatically that this is your vocation. You may also be called to the priesthood, to religious life as a brother or sister, or to the diaconate.
English:en.Finalmente, algunos siguen a Cristo en e: Finally, some follow Christ in the celibate state of life. Remember: it is normal to desire marriage and a family. That desire does not exclude the possibility that you may have a priestly vocation.
English:en.4. ¿Cómo puedo saber a qué me llama Dio:
4.  How can I know what God is calling me to?  
    You should pray every day asking God to reveal His plan to you. Don’t ask, “What do I want to do when I grow up?” but rather, “Jesus, what do You want me to do?” And listen for the answer!
English:en.El lugar principal donde Dios habla es e: The principal place where God speaks is the heart. Listen with the heart. Furthermore, the discernment of a priestly vocation is always done with the Church. The local bishop, assisted by the vocations office and the seminary, is the one who ultimately confirms the call.
English:en.5. ¿Puedo ser feliz si no sigo el plan:
5.  Can I be happy if I don’t follow God’s plan for me?  
    If you do not follow the vocation for which you were created, you may attain a certain happiness in this life and still be saved, but you will never be as fully happy as you would be if you followed your true vocation.
English:en.Por eso es tan importante discernir corr: That is why it is so important to discern correctly. Of course, every vocation has trials and difficulties, but there is also great joy in giving one’s life for Jesus.
English:en.6. ¿Son felices la mayoría de los sacer:
6.  Are most priests happy in their vocation?  
    Most priests are very happy! The priestly life is deeply fulfilling, both in this life and in the eternal one.
English:en.Los medios de comunicación a veces dan u: The media sometimes give a distorted image of priests, presenting them as frustrated or unhappy, or reducing them to the scandals of a few. That simply is not true.
English:en.7. ¿Por qué los sacerdotes no pueden ca:
7.  Why can’t priests get married? Isn’t that very difficult?  
    Priests of the Roman Rite do not marry in order to devote themselves entirely to Jesus and His people. They beget “spiritual children” by bringing many souls to Christ and helping them grow in holiness.
English:en.El celibato es un signo poderoso para el: Celibacy is a powerful sign to the world that only Jesus can give us the happiness we all long for. Renouncing marriage and family shows that Christ is real and worthy of giving one’s life for Him.
English:en.Toda vocación exige sacrificios, pero ha: Every vocation requires sacrifices, but there is great joy in sacrificing out of love. As one priest said: “It’s true that no one will call me ‘dad,’ but thousands call me ‘father.’”
English:en.8. ¿Alguna vez se permitirá que los sac:
8.  Will priests ever be allowed to marry?  
    Celibacy will always exist in the Church because it is not only a law, but a charism, a spiritual gift and a source of fruitfulness. Jesus was celibate. Saint Paul was as well.
English:en.El celibato ayuda al sacerdote a unirse: Celibacy helps the priest to unite more intimately with Christ and to be a sign of the Kingdom of God. Although there are some married priests in the Catholic Church, they are exceptional cases.
English:en.Si alguna vez el Espíritu Santo quisiera: If the Holy Spirit ever wanted a change, He would bring it about through the Pope and the bishops. But it would be a serious mistake to go to the seminary “hoping” that this will happen.
English:en.9. ¿Habría más sacerdotes si pudieran c:
9.  Would there be more priests if they could marry?  
    Perhaps, but the question is whether that would be best for the Church. Celibacy for the sake of the Kingdom is a powerful testimony that Jesus is real and worthy of total self-giving.
English:en.Además, las vocaciones sacerdotales han: Moreover, priestly vocations have grown enormously in recent decades in Africa, Asia, and Latin America, while they have declined in Europe and the United States. The problem is not celibacy, but deeper cultural and social factors.
English:en.10. ¿Estaré solo si me hago sacerdote?:
10.  Will I be lonely if I become a priest?  
    Loneliness is part of every vocation, but priests are rarely alone: they accompany people at the most important moments of their lives—birth, sacraments, marriage, death—and live surrounded by their community.
English:en.Cuando llega la soledad, Jesús llena ese: When loneliness comes, Jesus fills that emptiness, as He does in every vocation.
English:en.11. ¿Los sacerdotes reciben salario? \:
11.  Do priests receive a salary?  
    Yes, although they live a simple life. They receive what is necessary to cover their expenses, maintain a vehicle, rest, and carry out normal activities. In addition, the parish provides their lodging and food, so their expenses are minimal.
English:en.12. ¿Pueden divertirse los sacerdotes?:
12.  Can priests have fun?  
    Of course. Priests can play sports, watch movies, read, travel, go to the theater, fish, play soccer or golf, as long as it is consistent with the Christian life.
English:en.13. Si me atrae el sacerdocio, ¿signifi:
13.  If I’m attracted to the priesthood, does that mean I have a vocation?  
    Possibly, but not necessarily. It is important to pray, go to Mass, live a Christian life, and speak with a priest or the vocations director. They can help you discern clearly.
English:en.14. No soy muy “santo”. ¿Aun así puedo:
14.  I’m not very “holy.” Can I still be a priest?  
    Yes. Holiness is a lifelong journey. God forms His priests little by little. Use the sacrament of Confession, pray daily, and you will see how Christ transforms you.
English:en.15. ¿Es fácil llegar a ser sacerdote?:
15.  Is it easy to become a priest?  
    No. Serious formation is required: four years of college (including philosophy) and another four in the seminary to obtain a master’s degree in theology. But don’t be discouraged: the seminary is a wonderful experience and God gives the necessary grace.
English:en.16. ¿Es necesaria tanta educación? \n:
16.  Is so much education necessary?  
    Yes. The priest must be well formed in the humanities, theology, and spiritual life in order to serve with wisdom and be respected by the faithful. Every soul is precious and deserves a well-prepared shepherd.
English:en.17. ¿Qué cualidades busca la Iglesia en:
17.  What qualities does the Church look for in a candidate?  
    He should be a practicing Catholic, who prays every day, lives the commandments, serves others, enjoys good physical, mental, and emotional health, and has average or above-average intelligence. Above all, he must be open to God’s will.
English:en.18. Si entro al seminario “para probar”:
18.  If I enter the seminary “to try it out,” am I committing for life?  
    No. The seminary is precisely the place for discernment. Many men enter, try it for a time, and then choose another path. Even so, they leave strengthened in their faith.
English:en.19. ¿Es interesante la vida del sacerdo:
19.  Is the priest’s life interesting?  
    Very! Every day is different. There are challenges, but also a deep joy. At the end of the day, the priest can say: “Lord, today I have given myself for You.” There is no fuller way to live.
English:en.¿Responderás si Jesús te llama?: Will you answer if Jesus calls you?
English:en.## Dios no te hizo por casualidad: **te: ## God did not make you by chance: He dreamed you for something great. Your vocation is the unique way in which He wants to love you and for you to love Him.
English:en.**Atrévete a descubrirla… y a vivirla a: Dare to discover it… and live it to the full.
English:en.Pedir al dueño de la mies. Con rostros c: Ask the Lord of the harvest. With concrete faces
English:en.Todos caminamos juntos y **lo que vivimo:
We all walk together, and what we live of God is transmitted.  
Observe who can continue the self-giving and encourage others to follow this path.
English:en.¿Dios me está llamando al sacerdocio?: Is God calling me to the priesthood?
English:en.¿Y si Dios me llama a darle todo?: What if God is calling me to give Him everything?
English:en.¿Llevar a Cristo a todas las partes de l: Bringing Christ to every part of society?
English:en.Ordenar todo a Cristo desde dentro: Order everything to Christ from within
English:en.## **¿Cómo saber si Dios me llama? — Ven: ## **How to know if God is calling me? — Overcoming fear**
English:en.Quizá alguna vez haya pasado por tu ment: Perhaps the idea of the priesthood has crossed your mind at some point, but fear pushes it away:
English:en.* Miedo al compromiso\n* Miedo al ce:
*   Fear of commitment
*   Fear of celibacy
*   Fear of not being worthy
English:en.Estos temores son muy comunes, incluso e: These fears are very common, even among those already in the seminary. But literally thousands of men have had the same doubts and still became holy and dedicated priests.
English:en.La primera verdad que debes recordar es: The first truth you must remember is that **God does not speak through fear**. Fear is a weapon of the Enemy to turn you away from God’s will; it is like the bite of an animal that paralyzes its prey so it cannot move.
English:en.Un hombre dominado por el miedo tendrá d: A man dominated by fear will have difficulty moving forward toward God’s will.
English:en.Entonces, ¿cómo superar el miedo? Aquí t: So how do you overcome fear? Here are **five ideas**:
English:en.Puedes tener preocupaciones legítimas so: You may have legitimate concerns about celibacy or preaching, but that is not the same as being afraid. Instead of letting fear dominate you, look at the demands of the priesthood objectively. It is true that the priest needs self-control, affective maturity, and the ability to speak in public. These and other areas require a serious personal examination.
English:en.Sí, descubrirás cosas que debes mejorar,: Yes, you will discover things you need to improve, but do your discernment with a calm mind, not with a frightened heart.
English:en.Recuerda que cuando Jesús llamó a Pedro: Remember that when Jesus called Peter (Lk 5:1-11), the first Pope said to him:
English:en.> “Apártate de mí, Señor, que soy un pec: > “Depart from me, Lord, for I am a sinner.”
English:en.Y Jesús le aseguró que no debía tener mi: And Jesus assured him that he need not be afraid to follow Him. Likewise, Jesus knows your weaknesses and difficulties. If you trust in Him and “put out into the deep,” everything will be well.
English:en.El P. Brett Brannen, en su libro _Salvar: Fr. Brett Brannen, in his book _To Save a Thousand Souls_, recommends this meditation when a man feels afraid:
English:en.> “Dios es infinito en poder y me ama in: > “God is infinite in power and loves me infinitely. No one can snatch me from his hand. God will never send me where his grace cannot sustain me. If He asks me for something difficult, like being a priest, He will give me the grace to do it. I will not fail, because He is with me. I will be happy because I do His will. Even if I do not have all the necessary qualities, God will help me develop them. In His will is my peace.”
English:en.### **3) Confía tus miedos a la Virgen M: ### **3) Entrust your fears to the Virgin Mary.**
English:en.Recuerda que, después de que el ángel le: Remember that after the angel told her “do not be afraid,” Mary promptly accepted God’s will:
English:en.> “He aquí la esclava del Señor; hágase: > “Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it done unto me according to your word.”
English:en.Entrégale tus temores a su intercesión:: Entrust your fears to her intercession: she will calm your heart and help you discover the will of her Son.
English:en.### **4) Recuerda para qué es el seminar: ### **4) Remember what the seminary is for.**
English:en.Desde un punto de vista práctico, es con: From a practical point of view, it is consoling to know that if God is calling you to the priesthood, ordination is still a long way off. The seminary is a time of intense formation to face doubts, grow in holiness, and prepare for a fruitful ministry. **No man enters the seminary already ready to be a priest.**
English:en.### **5) Busca consuelo en la Palabra de: ### **5) Seek comfort in the Word of God.**
English:en.Si tienes miedo al sacerdocio, no estás: If you are afraid of the priesthood, you are not alone. The prophets and apostles also felt fear when they were called. Read the following Scripture passages to see how God encouraged them:
English:en.* **No soy lo bastante santo:** Is 6,1:
*   **I am not holy enough:** Is 6:1-9; Lk 5:1-11
*   **I fear failure:** Ex 14:10-31; Lk 15
*   **I have sinned:** Jn 21:15-23; Mt 9:9-13; Lk 7:36-50
*   **I am too young:** 1 Sam 3:1-18; 1 Sam 16:1-13; Jer 1:4-10; Lk 1:26-38
*   **I don’t have enough talent:** 1 Sam 17:32-51; Lk 1:26-38
*   **I want to have a family:** Gn 12:1-3; Mt 12:46-50; Mk 10:28-30
*   **I want “a good life”:** Mk 10:17-31; 1 Cor 2:9; Mt 13:44-46; Jn 10:10; Mt 16:24-27
*   **I’m afraid to commit forever:** Ruth 1:15-17; Mt 28:16-20; 1 Cor 12:5-10
*   **Public speaking scares me:** Ex 4:10-17; Jer 1:4-10
*   **I’m not smart enough:** 2 Cor 4:7-18; Ex 4:10-17
*   **I fear being alone:** Ex 3:4-22; Lk 1:28-38; Mt 28:20
*   **I want to be happy:** Ps 37:4; Mt 5:1-12; Jn 10:10; Mk 10:28-31; 1 Cor 2:9
*   **I am full of fear:** 1 Jn 4:18 “There is no fear in love; perfect love casts out fear.”
English:en.Como recordaba con frecuencia san Juan P: As St. John Paul II often reminded us: **Do not be afraid!** Do not let fear paralyze you. Speak with a priest you trust about your concerns.
English:en.Permanece fiel a la oración diaria, conf: Remain faithful to daily prayer, trust in God, and your heart will be at peace, whatever your vocation may be.
English:en.## **Ministerios laicales: La Iglesia cu: ## **Lay ministries: The Church counts on you**
English:en.### **Todos estamos llamados. Todos pode: ### **We are all called. We can all serve.**
English:en.La misión de la Iglesia no es sólo de lo: The mission of the Church is not only for priests. Every baptized faithful has a unique place in the history of salvation. Through their faith, dedication, and commitment, the laity participate in Christ’s mission: **priestly, prophetic, and kingly**, each according to their gifts and circumstances.
English:en.Ser laico no significa ser espectador. S: Being a layperson does not mean being a spectator. It means **taking the faith and living it in daily life**. The laity are called to act as **light in the world**, transforming, **from the spirit of Christ**,
English:en.* Sobre todo, la vida familiar y profe: *   Above all, family and professional life.
English:en.> Cuantos más laicos compenetrados con e: > The more lay people permeated with the evangelical spirit there are, the more the world will open itself to the Kingdom of God.
English:en.Los laicos pueden participar directament: Laypeople can participate directly in the life of the Church in many ways. **It is not necessary to be a priest** to contribute to the liturgy or to pastoral care, always under the guidance of the shepherds.
English:en.* Proclamar la Palabra de Dios en la e: *   Proclaim the Word of God at the Eucharist
English:en.* Dar la Sagrada Comunión cuando los m: *   Give Holy Communion when the ministers cannot
