English:en.Estas tareas, aunque importantes, **no h: These tasks, though important, do not make the layperson a priest. Their legitimacy comes from pastoral authority and their sacramental root is Baptism and Confirmation, not ordination.
English:en.### **Ministerios laicales: santos en lo: ### Lay ministries: saints in the everyday
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\">Cada lec: Each reader, minister of Communion, catechist, or participant in any lay ministry not only fulfills a function, but is called to be holy in their service.
English:en.La verdadera eficacia de su labor no dep: The true effectiveness of their work does not depend solely on technique or formal compliance, but on a life offered and coherent with the faith. Being a reader or a catechist means preparing the heart, cultivating prayer, and living the Gospel so that every word, every gesture, and every encounter may convey the presence of Christ.
English:en.<p style=\"text-align: center\">El minis: Lay ministry thus becomes a path of personal sanctification: it is not enough to transmit knowledge, distribute the Eucharist, or guide in catechesis if one does not first live one’s own faith with authenticity.
English:en.La santidad del laico transforma su mini: The holiness of the layperson transforms their ministry, making it a light for others and true support for priests. Every act of service, however small it may seem, is a living testimony of God’s love and an example that inspires the whole community.
English:en.> Ser laico que sirve es ser santo en lo: > To be a layperson who serves is to be holy in the ordinary, reflecting the Gospel not only in the function, but in the whole of life.
English:en.No solo rezar: buscar: Not only to pray: to seek
English:en.## **No solo rezar, buscar**: ## Not only to pray, to seek
English:en.### **La vocación no nace de la nada**: ### Vocation does not arise from nothing
English:en.A veces parece que las vocaciones —al sa:
Sometimes it seems that vocations—to the priesthood, to marriage, or to consecrated life— arise out of nowhere, as if God called in secret without anyone else having a part.  
But it is not so: God calls through concrete faces, living communities, people who truly care.  
Therefore, it is not enough to pray for vocations. We have to seek them.
English:en.### **Rezar sí… pero con los ojos abiert: ### Pray, yes… but with eyes open
English:en.Jesús lo dijo con claridad: _“La mies es:
Jesus said it clearly: “The harvest is plentiful and the laborers are few; pray therefore the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest” (Mt 9:37-38).  
That prayer is not passive or theoretical. It is a call to make us ourselves collaborators of God, those who help others to discover their path.
English:en.Rezar por las vocaciones significa **mir:
To pray for vocations means to look around: at the young people in your parish, your friends, the one who serves with joy, the one who listens with patience, the one who lives the faith with coherence.  
When you see someone like that, do not keep silent. It may be time to ask, with simplicity and trust:
English:en.> “¿Has pensado alguna vez que Dios podr: > “Have you ever thought that God might be calling you to something more?”
English:en.A veces una pregunta como esa, lanzada p: Sometimes a question like that, posed by a priest, a catechist, or a believing friend, can awaken a sleeping vocation.
English:en.### **Cada uno busca la santidad del otr: ### Each one seeks the holiness of the other
English:en.La vocación no es un asunto privado. **T:
Vocation is not a private matter. We are all responsible for the holiness of the other.  
A layperson who encourages, a mother who prays, a priest who proposes, a community that accompanies… all take part in the birth of new vocations.
English:en.El mundo necesita **buenos esposos**, **: The world needs good spouses, good priests, good consecrated women. And that begins when someone dares to look with faith at the qualities of the other:
English:en.> “Sería un padre entregado.” \n> “Podr:
> “He would be a devoted father.”  
> “She could be a great sister.”  
> “He has the soul of a shepherd.”
English:en.No se trata de etiquetar, sino de **reco: It is not about labeling, but about recognizing God’s action in people and helping them discern it.
English:en.Mira con atención. **Apunta en el corazó:
Look attentively. Write in your heart the names of those who convey joy, hope, service.  
Accompany them with your friendship, with your prayer, with your example.  
And pray each day:
English:en.> “Señor, haz que cada uno descubra su c: > “Lord, let each one discover their path, and may we know how to accompany one another in discernment.”
English:en.Ninguna vocación florece en solitario. *:
No vocation flourishes in isolation. It arises in a community that prays, that listens, that walks together.  
Synodality—this walking together—is not only a pastoral method: it is the very way in which God calls.  
Each vocation, each story, is the fruit of a people that accompanies, proposes, and sustains.
English:en.Noviazgo y Seminario. Épocas de discerni: Courtship and Seminary. Times of discernment
English:en.Pedid al dueño de la mies: Ask the Lord of the harvest
English:en.Jesús lo dijo con verdad: **hay mucho tr:
Jesus said it truly: there is much work to be done and few hands willing to do it.  
Souls—that is the harvest— await pastors who will accompany them, heal them, and lead them to God.  
But if priests are lacking, there are lacking those who bring the Gospel, who celebrate the sacraments, who announce the forgiveness and mercy of Christ.
English:en.No se trata solo de una escasez numérica:
It is not only a numerical shortage: it is an urgency of the heart of the Church.  
There is hunger for God, thirst for meaning, a need for pastors.  
And Christ himself has told us how to respond: by asking.
English:en.Jesús no dijo “organizad más” o “buscad: Jesus did not say “organize more” or “seek better methods.” He said:
English:en.> **“Rogad al Dueño de la mies.”**: > “Pray to the Lord of the harvest.”
English:en.La vocación sacerdotal **no nace de una:
The priestly vocation does not arise from a strategy, but from prayer.  
God continues to call, but he waits for his people to intercede.  
When the Church prays, the Holy Spirit acts; when a family prays, a heart can hear the call.
English:en.Por eso, **cada cristiano tiene una resp:
Therefore, each Christian has a real responsibility:  
to ask the Lord of the harvest to send holy, generous, joyful laborers.
English:en.No basta con pedir una vez. Jesús habló:
It is not enough to ask once. Jesus spoke of praying without losing heart.  
Priestly vocations are born in a Church that prays with insistence and trust.
English:en.Reza cada día, reza en familia, reza el:
Pray every day, pray as a family, pray the Rosary asking for priests and for those who could become priests.  
Each Hail Mary is a seed.  
And how many priests, when telling their story, confess it:
English:en.> “Mi abuela rezaba el Rosario por las v:
> “My grandmother prayed the Rosary for vocations.”  
> “A group in my parish asked each week for new priests.”  
> “Someone, in silence, was praying for me.”
English:en.**Nada se pierde.** Cada oración mueve e: Nothing is lost. Each prayer moves the heart of God.
English:en.Pedir vocaciones es un acto de fe, pero:
Asking for vocations is an act of faith, but also of generosity.  
It means recognizing that the Church does not belong to us: it belongs to Christ.  
And he wants his harvest to have laborers, his people to have pastors.
English:en.Dedicar tiempo, oración, sacrificio, inc:
Dedicating time, prayer, sacrifice, even offering a Rosary or a Communion for vocations, is part of our mission as laypeople, families, and communities.  
There is no prayer more fruitful than the one that asks for those who one day will bring the grace of God to so many souls.
English:en.La vocación nace en una **comunidad que:
Vocation is born in a community that prays, that walks together, that lives the synodality of knowing itself responsible for all.  
It is not enough to hope that “others” will pray.  
Each one counts. Each one can be an instrument so that a young person may hear the voice of Christ who calls:
English:en.La mies es mucha. Las almas esperan. \n:
The harvest is plentiful. Souls are waiting.  
And the Lord has given us the way: to ask, to trust, to persevere.
English:en.Pide cada día al Dueño de la mies. \nHa:
Ask the Lord of the harvest every day.  
Do it with faith, with constancy, with love for the Church.  
Because God listens. And when the Church prays, **He answers.**
English:en.### Dos caminos, una misma entrega: la v: ### Two paths, one and the same total self-gift: the active life and the contemplative life
English:en.Cuando alguien pregunta _“¿qué hacen las:
When someone asks _“what do nuns do?”_, the most honest answer would be:  
**“they love, serve, and pray for the world.”**  
But behind those three words lies **an entire life of self-giving**.  
Some do it **in silence, within walls**, and others **amid the noise of the world**.  
Both forms are two faces of the same love: **Christ**.
English:en.> “Las monjas son el rostro femenino de: > “Nuns are the feminine face of a Church that prays, serves, and loves.”
English:en.## Monjas de clausura: el corazón oculto: ## Cloistered nuns: the hidden heart that sustains everyone
English:en.Son las **monjas contemplativas**, las q:
They are the **contemplative nuns**, those who live “hidden with Christ in God.”  
From the silence of their monasteries, **they pray for all**, for those who believe and those who do not, for those who suffer, for those who love, for the whole world.
English:en.### Así late su día: ### This is how their day beats
English:en.Su vida está marcada por un ritmo sereno: Their life is marked by a serene and steady rhythm:
English:en.* **Silencio**, que no es vacío, sino: *   **Silence**, which is not emptiness, but **an encounter with God**.
English:en.> “El silencio no las separa del mundo,: > “Silence does not separate them from the world; it unites them to it from within.”
English:en.Aunque viven en clausura, **no están des:
Although they live in enclosure, **they are not disconnected**.  
They welcome visitors in the parlor, pray for specific intentions, and some make products they sell to support their community.  
In a world that runs, they **stop so that love can keep breathing**.
English:en.## Monjas de vida activa: las manos visi: ## Active-life nuns: the visible hands of the Gospel
English:en.Mientras las contemplativas oran en el s:
While contemplatives pray in silence, **active-life nuns** go out to meet the world.  
They are the ones who **teach, care, accompany, heal, and evangelize**.  
Their prayer becomes **action**, their cloister is **the heart of their neighbor**.
English:en.* En **colegios y universidades**, for: *   In **schools and universities**, forming minds and hearts.
English:en.* En **hospitales**, atendiendo enferm: *   In **hospitals**, tending the sick with tenderness.
English:en.* En **barrios humildes**, compartiend: *   In **humble neighborhoods**, sharing life with the poorest.
English:en.* En **misiones lejanas**, anunciando: *   In **distant missions**, proclaiming Christ with a smile.
English:en.* En **parroquias**, acompañando grupo: *   In **parishes**, accompanying groups, youth, families, the elderly.
English:en.> “Mientras unas oran por el mundo, otra: > “While some pray for the world, others embrace it with their hands. But both love it with the same love.”
English:en.Aunque vivan de formas distintas, **toda:
Although they live in different ways, **all nuns have the same mission**:  
**to follow Christ more closely and make His love present.**  
Some do it through contemplation; others, through action.  
But both are **invisible pillars** that uphold the Body of the Church.
English:en.> “Si la Iglesia es un cuerpo, las monja:
> “If the Church is a body, cloistered nuns are the heart that prays,  
> and active-life nuns are the hands that serve.”
English:en.Las contemplativas recuerdan que Dios es:
The contemplatives remind us that God is the center.  
The active remind us that **the love of God translates into works**.  
And together they teach us that **faith cannot be understood without prayer or without charity**.
English:en.## 💬 ¿Por qué elegir una vida así?: ## 💬 Why choose a life like this?
English:en.Porque en un mundo que busca éxito, ella:
Because in a world that seeks success, they choose **to give their lives**.  
In a time of noise, they **choose silence or service**.  
And in a society that runs aimlessly, they **stop to gaze at Christ**.
English:en.Ser monja —de clausura o de vida activa—:
To be a nun—cloistered or active—is **a vocation of radical love**:  
a total “yes” to God, a lifelong commitment to the Gospel.
English:en.> “No importa si oran o enseñan, si coci:
> “It doesn’t matter if they pray or teach, if they cook or go on mission:  
> everything in them proclaims that God is worth one’s entire life.”
English:en.## Dos estilos, una misma melodía: ## Two styles, one and the same melody
English:en.En los monasterios o en las calles, \ne:
In monasteries or on the streets,  
in silence or amid the cries of the world,  
**nuns remain witnesses that God is alive**.
English:en.Y mientras el mundo corre buscando senti:
And while the world runs searching for meaning,  
they—through prayer or action—remind us of the deepest secret:  
**happiness is found in giving oneself completely.**
English:en.**Un Ministerio Sagrado al Pueblo de Dio: **A Sacred Ministry to the People of God** _"The faithful expect only one thing from priests: that they be specialists in promoting the encounter between man and God. They are not asked to be experts in economics, construction, or politics. They are expected to be experts in the spiritual life."_ ~ Pope Benedict XVI
English:en.**¿Podría Dios estar llamándote a ser sa: **Could God be calling you to be a priest?** God made you for a unique purpose. Even before you were born, He knew your vocation, your mission in life. And if you are a faithful Catholic man, God may be calling you to a higher purpose: to become a Catholic priest.
English:en.Los sacerdotes tienen una misión crucial: Priests have a crucial mission: to bring people to Jesus and to bring Jesus to people. They are spiritual fathers to thousands of Catholics. They preach the Gospel and offer the sacrifice of the Mass. In short, priests are living witnesses of Christ in the world: men of firm character who stand out in our secular culture.
English:en.Un buen sacerdote es un héroe espiritual: A good priest is a spiritual hero, a man who sacrifices himself for the people of God. Is Jesus calling you to be His priest?
English:en.**El sacerdocio es una vocación.** Crist: **The priesthood is a vocation.** Christ asks some men for the sacrifice of their lives to follow Him as His closest companions. From all eternity, certain men are called to the priesthood.
English:en.Es un llamado inscrito en su naturaleza: It is a call inscribed in their nature and, therefore, it is a call that will bring them fulfillment.
**A priest acts In Persona Christi.** Priests act _in persona Christi capitas_, which means “in the person of Christ, head of the Church.” That is why the priest speaks in the first person at Mass: “This is my body, given up for you.”
English:en.Como escribió el Papa Juan Pablo II: _“E: As Pope John Paul II wrote: _“The priest offers his humanity to Christ, so that Christ may use him as an instrument of salvation, making him, so to speak, another Christ.”_
English:en.**Un sacerdote posee un Poder Sagrado.**: **A priest possesses a Sacred Power.** When a priest makes the sacraments present, he possesses a sacred power granted by God, in Latin, _sacra potestas_.
English:en.**El alma de un sacerdote cambia para si: **A priest’s soul is changed forever.** At ordination, a man’s soul undergoes an ontological change—a change of being—that marks his soul indelibly forever. Once a priest, always a priest.
English:en.El noviazgo es un tiempo para **conocers: Courtship is a time to **get to know each other, interact, and respect each other**. It is not a simple stage prior to marriage, but an opportunity to learn to truly love. The Church invites us to live it with depth and joy, because it is the natural path to discern the vocation to conjugal love and to prepare for a total and faithful self-giving.
English:en.## Conocerse: descubrir al otro para ama: ## Getting to know each other: discovering the other to love better
English:en.El noviazgo es un tiempo de conocimiento: Courtship is a time of mutual knowledge. In it, two people gradually discover each other: their character, their dreams, their faith, their way of facing life. Whoever has been called by God to marriage needs to know well the person with whom they are going to share their life. The greater the knowledge, the lower the risk of being mistaken.
English:en.Por eso, la Iglesia desea que antes del: For this reason, the Church desires that before marriage there be a time of courtship, to get to know each other more and thus love each other better. This knowledge is not reduced to external data, but implies discovering the other’s heart: their faith, their values, their wounds, and their virtues.
English:en.Amar no es dejarse llevar solo por el se: To love is not to be carried away only by feeling. True love integrates **feeling, intelligence, and will**. Feeling kindles, intelligence enlightens, and the will sustains. Love is not improvised: it is learned, cultivated, and decided each day.
English:en.## Tratarse: compartir la vida con since: ## Interacting: sharing life with sincerity and faith
English:en.No se puede conocer sin trato, ni amar s: One cannot know without interaction, nor love without sharing. The interaction between those dating should be deep and sincere, covering essential topics: faith, the vision of the family, children, work, values… Courtship cannot be based on appearances or passing emotions, but on the search for the truth of the other.
English:en.Los novios cristianos se tratan con resp: Christian couples treat each other with respect and patience, knowing that both are fragile and that love demands forgiveness and understanding. It is not about waiting for the other to change, but about learning to love them as they are, and discerning whether both are walking in the same direction.
English:en.El trato también implica reconocer si ha: Interaction also entails recognizing whether there is **affective maturity**. Immaturity is shown when one lives guided by one’s moods, does not know how to wait, needs to draw attention, or fears committing. A Christian courtship helps one grow in self-mastery, to leave space for the other, and to cultivate a free and sincere relationship.
English:en.## Respetarse: amar con pureza y liberta: ## Respecting each other: loving with purity and freedom
English:en.Respetarse es reconocer que el otro es u: To respect each other is to recognize that the other is a gift, not a possession. Mutual respect is expressed in delicacy, temperance, and chastity. Chastity is not a denial of love, but its noblest defense: it protects the heart and teaches one to love without using the other.
English:en.El Papa Francisco recuerda que el noviaz: Pope Francis reminds us that courtship is “a time of waiting and preparation, which must be lived in the chastity of gestures and words.” That waiting strengthens love, makes it free, mature, and capable of total self-giving. A love forever cannot be built on the pursuit of immediate pleasure.
English:en.Respetarse es también cuidar la comunica: Respecting each other also means taking care of communication, avoiding emotional blackmail, and not putting the other in extreme situations. Authentic love always seeks the good of the beloved, not one’s own satisfaction. Thus one learns to love as Christ loved: with patience, with self-gift, and without expecting anything in return.
English:en.Podemos decir que el amor humano tiene t: We can say that human love has three phases, which do not replace each other, but are integrated:
English:en.1. **Atracción:** surge espontáneamente: 1.  **Attraction:** it arises spontaneously. Someone seems attractive to you; interest is born and the desire to get to know them.
English:en.2. **Enamoramiento:** los sentimientos: 2.  **Infatuation:** feelings are on the surface. One enjoys the other’s presence and dreams of a future together.
English:en.3. **Amor decidido:** es la fase más ma: 3.  **Decided love:** it is the most mature phase. To love is no longer just to feel, but to **will to love**: to decide to do so every day, even when it is hard.
English:en.El sentimiento no se elige, pero el amor: Feeling is not chosen, but love is. To love is a decision that involves sacrifice, fidelity, and will. When one loves in this way, the feeling returns stronger and deeper, because gratuitous love—given without expecting anything in return—generates joy and fullness.
English:en.El noviazgo cristiano es un camino para: Christian courtship is a path to learn to love like Christ: with freedom, tenderness, and self-giving. It is not about seeking perfection, but about walking together toward it, supported by God’s grace.
English:en.Vivir un noviazgo así es preparar los ci:
To live courtship in this way is to prepare the foundations of a solid marriage. As Pope Francis says, _“living together is an art, a patient, beautiful and fascinating path that has rules that can be summarized in three words: May I?, Thank you, Forgive me.”_  
Three words that also summarize a holy courtship: respect, gratitude, and constant reconciliation.
English:en.**Ora et labora** —reza y trabaja—. \nE:
**Ora et labora** —pray and work—.  
That is the formula that sums up the whole life of the monk. But behind those three words there is a world: a way of life centered on **the search for God**, in the silence of the monastery and in the peace of a routine that is offered as prayer.
English:en.El **monje** vive para **contemplar a Di:
The **monk** lives to **contemplate God**. He does not flee from the world, but embraces it from the heart, interceding for the needs of all people.  
Each day, his prayer invisibly sustains the Church.  
His vocation is **contemplative**, but not useless: monks draw down grace upon the world with their silent fidelity.
English:en.### El ritmo del día: oración, lectura y: ### The rhythm of the day: prayer, reading, and work
English:en.El día del monje gira como un reloj sagr: The monk’s day turns like a sacred clock around three axes:
English:en.1. **Oración** – comunitaria y personal:
1.  **Prayer** – communal and personal.  
    Monks pray the **Divine Office**, the liturgical “hours” that mark the day:
English:en.* **Tercia, Sexta y Nona**, en medio d: *   **Terce, Sext, and None**, in the midst of work.
English:en.Y en el centro de todo: la **Eucaristía*: And at the center of everything: the **Eucharist**, the living heart of the monastery.
English:en.2. **Lectio divina** – la lectura orant:
2.  **Lectio divina** – the prayerful reading of the Word.  
    It is not academic study, but **listening to the Spirit**. From there sprang the great Benedictine libraries that preserved Christian and Greco-Roman culture for centuries.
English:en.3. **Trabajo** – manual o intelectual,:
3.  **Work** – manual or intellectual, always offered to God.  
    In an abbey, there may be bakers, cobblers, gardeners, bookbinders, musicians, librarians, or teachers.  
    Everything is done in obedience, in silence, and with love.
English:en.### Ora et labora: una oración hecha tra: ### Ora et labora: a prayer made work
English:en.En el monasterio **no hay horas vacías**:
In the monastery **there are no empty hours**. Each thing—singing the psalms, repairing a shoe, or copying a book—is a form of prayer.  
Work sustains the monastery and enables works of charity.  
The balance between prayer, work, and study keeps the monk **at peace, serene, and centered on God**.
English:en.De la atención amorosa a la **lectio div:
From the loving attention to **lectio divina**, monks naturally moved on to **study**.  
Therefore, the medieval abbeys were **beacons of light** in dark times:  
they copied manuscripts, taught people to read, and sowed the foundation of European culture.
English:en.Aunque “monje” viene del griego _monos_:
Although “monk” comes from the Greek _monos_ (alone), the monk **does not live isolated**, but **in community**.  
Under the guidance of an abbot, all seek “**to have one heart and one soul in God**,” as Saint Augustine says.  
The monastery is a family where each brother helps the other to reach heaven.
English:en.Los **monjes** viven según la **Regla de:
**Monks** live according to the **Rule of Saint Benedict**, centered on contemplation.  
**Friars** (Franciscans, Dominicans, Carmelites…) also consecrated, go out into the world to preach and serve.  
And **clerics regular** (such as the Jesuits) dedicate their lives to apostolate and teaching.  
Each path is different, but all seek the same thing: **God in all things**.
English:en.### En palabras de San Benito: ### In the words of Saint Benedict
> “Prefer nothing whatever to the love of Christ.”  
> — _Rule of Saint Benedict, ch. 4_
English:en.**Los sacerdotes hacen mucho más que cel:
**Priests do much more than celebrate Mass and pray all day.**  
A priest’s life is active, demanding, challenging… and deeply rewarding.
English:en.Existen muchos ministerios especializado: There are many specialized ministries, such as being a chaplain, working in archdiocesan offices, or teaching in a seminary. But most priests serve in parishes.
English:en.La gran mayoría de los sacerdotes viven: The vast majority of priests live happy and fulfilling lives. They love the people they serve and, in turn, are loved by their parishioners. Traditionally, priests are considered to fulfill the biblical role of **priest, prophet, and king.**
English:en.Los sacerdotes instruyen a otros en la f: Priests instruct others in the faith.
English:en.Los sacerdotes guían, unen y animan a su: Priests guide, unite, and encourage their parishioners.
English:en.Los sacerdotes administran los sacrament: Priests administer the sacraments and foster holiness.
English:en.* Celebrar la Misa y predicar el Evang: *   Celebrating Mass and preaching the Gospel.
English:en.* Incorporar nuevos miembros a la Igle: *   Bringing new members into the Church through Baptism.
English:en.* Escuchar confesiones y ofrecer direc: *   Hearing confessions and offering spiritual direction.
English:en.* Ayudar a adolescentes y jóvenes adul: *   Helping teens and young adults come to know Christ.
English:en.* Preparar a las parejas para el matri: *   Preparing couples for marriage and accompanying them in their married life.
English:en.* Alimentar a los pobres, visitar cárc: *   Feeding the poor, visiting prisons, and promoting justice.
English:en.* Acompañar a quienes pasan por moment: *   Accompanying those going through difficult times.
English:en.* Visitar a los enfermos en los hospit: *   Visiting the sick in hospitals and anointing them for their healing.
English:en.* Celebrar funerales y dar sepultura a: *   Celebrating funerals and burying the deceased.
English:en.* Administrar los asuntos materiales d: *   Administering the material affairs of the parish.
English:en.* Orar diariamente por el Pueblo de Di: *   Praying daily for the People of God.
English:en.Relaciones antes del matrimonio: amar si: Relationships before marriage: love without possessing
English:en.## Relaciones antes del matrimonio: amar: ## Relationships before marriage: love without possessing
English:en.El amor no se mide por la intensidad del:
Love is not measured by the intensity of desire, but by the truth of self-giving.  
In an age that confuses loving with using, and passion with happiness, many hearts end up broken because they skip God’s plan. Not because He wants to deprive us of something, but because He loves us so much that He only wants to give us what truly makes us free, pure, and happy.
English:en.## Amar no es poseer: ## To love is not to possess
English:en.Muchos piensan que el amor es tener al o: Many think that love is to have the other, to make them one’s own, to merge in every possible way. But Christian love does not seek to possess, but to **give and care**. Liking is wanting for myself; loving is willing the good of the other, even above my own desires.
English:en.Cuando una relación se vuelve posesiva,: When a relationship becomes possessive, when jealousy, control, or distrust appear, something has gone wrong. The human heart was made to love in freedom, and where there is fear or domination, love withers.
English:en.Los celos son muchas veces la señal de u: Jealousy is often the sign of a relationship that has lost its purity or its balance: because the body was given before the soul, or because love was confused with dependency. To love well requires learning to **wait, respect, and trust**.
English:en.## El amor se destruye cuando se adelant: ## Love is destroyed when it is rushed
English:en.La sexualidad es un don precioso de Dios: Sexuality is a precious gift from God, but it has its place: marriage. Not because pleasure is bad, but because the body has a language that says “I give myself completely, forever.” When that promise is spoken without the soul being ready, without having sealed it before God, something breaks inside.
English:en.**El sexo antes del matrimonio no une: c:
**Sex before marriage does not unite: it confuses.**  
It makes one believe there is intimacy where there is not yet, and it leaves wounds that drag on for years: feelings of emptiness, pain, dependency, or distrust.
English:en.El cuerpo se entrega antes de que la vol: The body is given before the will is mature, and then the soul rebels, because it was created for a total and definitive union. That is why so many relationships wear out, fade, or become torment.
English:en.Donde hay pecado, hay sufrimiento. No co: Where there is sin, there is suffering. Not as a punishment, but as a consequence. Because love is made for the light, not for the darkness.
English:en.## La castidad: la gran escuela del amor: ## Chastity: the great school of love
English:en.Ser casto no es reprimir el amor, sino *: Being chaste is not repressing love, but **learning to truly love**. Chastity orders the heart so that love may be pure, joyful, and faithful.
English:en.Los novios que luchan por la pureza no v: Dating couples who strive for purity do not live less love: they live it better. Their affection is stronger, their joy purer, their trust more solid. There are no shadows, no fear, no shame.
English:en.Como enseña el Catecismo de la Iglesia C: As the Catechism of the Catholic Church teaches:
English:en.> “Los novios están llamados a vivir la: “Engaged couples are called to live chastity in continence. In this trial they should see a discovery of mutual respect and an apprenticeship in fidelity. They will reserve for marriage the expressions of tenderness specific to conjugal love.”
English:en.Cada esfuerzo, cada renuncia, cada vez q: Every effort, every renunciation, every time one says “no” to impulse and “yes” to God, a seed is sown that will bear fruit in the future marriage. **You reap what you sow.**
English:en.El respeto es el guardián de la felicida: Respect is the guardian of the spouses’ happiness. The cleaner they arrive at the altar, the deeper their union. Those who have lived it say so: couples who overcome themselves and keep themselves arrive with new excitement, freshness, and tenderness.
English:en.La pasión tiene su lugar, pero cuando se:
Passion has its place, but when used without control, it ends up tiring. **Nothing used without measure remains beautiful.**  
Therefore, learning to wait is the best investment in love.
English:en.El noviazgo no es un matrimonio en peque: Dating is not a small-scale marriage. There is not yet a right to total self-giving. It is a time to get to know each other, to grow together, to prepare the soul and the heart.
English:en.## Amar con cabeza y con fe: ## Love with reason and with faith
English:en.El amor no necesita probarse con el cuer: Love does not need to be proven with the body, but demonstrated with fidelity, respect, care, and prayer. One who truly loves does not pressure. One who loves, waits.
English:en.Si uno de los dos no está viviendo su fe: If one of the two is not living their faith or does not understand what it means to be a child of God, they must be helped with patience, but also with firmness. To love is not to permit what destroys, but to **invite the other to the best of themselves**.
English:en.Puede que necesitéis un tiempo de distan: You may need a time of distance, to pray, clarify feelings, and heal. It’s okay. If the love is true, it will move forward; if not, it is better to let it go than to lose peace.
English:en.Y si necesitáis luz, **buscad consejo en: And if you need light, **seek counsel from a good priest**. Go to confession, pray together, receive the sacraments. With God’s grace, everything is possible. Without it, nothing endures.
English:en.Vivir la pureza también pasa por la form: Living purity also involves the way you present yourself. It’s not about being covered up or hiding, but about dressing with elegance, modesty, and respect for oneself and for others. Beauty lies in dignity, not in provocation.
English:en.Una mirada, un gesto, una palabra pueden: A look, a gesture, a word can ignite passions that should not burn. That’s why we must be prudent. Avoid solitary places, ambiguous moments, dark environments. Light protects love.
English:en.## Si te cuesta, no te rindas: ## If it’s hard for you, don’t give up
English:en.Ser puro no es fácil, pero es posible. R: Being pure is not easy, but it is possible. It requires will, prayer, and the sacraments. And the reward is great: an inner peace that no pleasure can match.
English:en.Hay novios que lo consiguen. Muchos. Que: There are couples who achieve it. Many. Who live their love with respect and joy, and who arrive at marriage with a clean happiness. They are a testimony that **purity does not take away love: it elevates it**.
English:en.Y si has caído, **no te quedes ahí**. Di: And if you have fallen, **don’t stay there**. God never tires of forgiving. Start again, trust, and let His mercy renew your heart.
English:en.Tu novio no es todavía tu esposo. El amo: Your boyfriend is not yet your husband. Total love belongs to marriage. Do not be deceived by those who tell you that “it doesn’t matter,” that “everyone does it.” To love with purity is to swim against the current, but whoever does so finds true joy.
English:en.Defiende tu castidad con entereza. Haz d: Defend your chastity with fortitude. Make your love story a beautiful and clean testimony, something you can one day tell your children without shame, with your heart full of gratitude.
Your purity is not fragility, it is strength.  
Your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit, not an instrument of desire.  
Your soul is for God.
English:en.Y recuerda: **más vale entrar sola en el: And remember: **it is better to enter the Kingdom of Heaven alone than accompanied on the wrong path.**
English:en.## María, modelo de pureza y ternura: ## Mary, model of purity and tenderness
English:en.Mira a María, Virgen y Madre. En Ella se: Look to Mary, Virgin and Mother. In her, delicacy and strength, tenderness and firmness come together. Mary teaches that purity is not coldness, but true love.
English:en.Su ejemplo ha sostenido a generaciones e: Her example has supported entire generations of young people who chose to love well, at all costs. Look also to the saints who defended their purity with courage: Maria Goretti, Josefina Vilaseca… They are proof that it is worth it.
English:en.¡La mejor formación que ofrece la Iglesi: The best formation the Church offers!
English:en.El **seminario** es el lugar donde un ho:
The **seminary** is the place where a man is formed—mind, body, and soul—into the image of **Jesus Christ**.  
It is not a place where seminarians walk in silence all day singing in Latin; on the contrary, it is a **place of joy, fraternity, and deep learning**.  
Today’s seminarians receive the best formation the Church offers!
English:en.> “La formación de los futuros sacerdote:
“The formation of future priests… is considered by the Church as one of the most demanding and important tasks for the future of the evangelization of humanity.”  
— _St. John Paul II, Pastores Dabo Vobis_
English:en.¿Ser laico es una vocación?: Is being a layperson a vocation?
English:en.### **El Pueblo de Dios y la Vocación La: ### **The People of God and the Lay Vocation**
English:en.El **Pueblo de Dios** está compuesto, en: The **People of God** is composed, for the most part, of **lay faithful**: all the **baptized who have not received the sacrament of Holy Orders nor made religious vows**. Christ calls them as the **Church**, not only to be recipients of the Good News of Salvation, but also to be **active agents of evangelization** in the world. Each baptized person is invited to sow the Gospel in the “vineyard of God,” which is the society in which they live, exercising an **indispensable evangelizing task**.
English:en.Como nos recuerda el Señor en el Evangel: As the Lord reminds us in the Gospel:
English:en.> “Id también vosotros a mi viña” (Mt. 2:
“You too go into my vineyard” (Mt. 20:3–4)  
“Go into all the world and proclaim the Good News to the whole creation” (Mk. 16:15; cf. ChL 33)
English:en.A través del **Bautismo**, los fieles so: Through **Baptism**, the faithful are **grafted into Christ** and receive a **threefold vocation**:
English:en.1. **Sacerdotal**: ofrecer la propia vi: 1.  **Priestly**: to offer one’s life as a spiritual sacrifice, sanctifying daily life through acts of love and service.
English:en.2. **Profética**: ser testigos de la ve: 2.  **Prophetic**: to be witnesses to the truth of Christ in words and actions, proclaiming the Gospel consistently in every sphere of life.
English:en.3. **Real**: participar en la construcc: 3.  **Royal**: to participate in building society according to the values of the Kingdom of God, being leaven of justice and peace in public life.
English:en.Según **Lumen Gentium**, los laicos está: According to **Lumen Gentium**, the laity are called to **seek the Kingdom of God in their daily life**, ordering temporal affairs according to the spirit of Jesus Christ. They act as **“leaven” that sanctifies the world from within**, participating in the mission of the Church in **social, family, and professional** life. This vocation **must be constantly fostered** by pastors in the particular Churches, helping the laity to discover their identity and mission in the world.
English:en.Cada laico está llamado a **transformar: Each layperson is called to **transform his or her environment**, bringing faith to every dimension of life:
English:en.* En el **trabajo**, siendo testimonio: *   In **work**, bearing witness to honesty, excellence, and service.
English:en.* En la **política y la economía**, pr: *   In **politics and economics**, promoting justice, solidarity, and the common good.
English:en.* En la **ciencia, el arte y la cultur: *   In **science, art, and culture**, illuminating creativity with Christian values.
English:en.* En la **familia y la comunidad**, se: *   In **family and community**, sowing love, respect, and fraternity.
English:en.Ser laico no es secundario ni pasivo; es: Being a layperson is neither secondary nor passive; it is **an active and concrete vocation** that requires formation, prayer, and commitment. Every Christian, in daily life, is called to **be a sign of the Kingdom of God in the world**, fulfilling his or her mission of evangelization from his or her own reality and exercising the threefold **priestly, prophetic, and royal** function, as Lumen Gentium teaches.
English:en.## **¿Cómo saber si Dios me está llamand: ## **How do I know if God is calling me?**
English:en.Si alguna vez has pensado en ser sacerdo: If you have ever thought about being a priest, these signs can help you discover whether God is calling you to follow Him more closely:
English:en.### **2\\. Dios ha puesto en tu corazón: ### **2. God has placed in your heart the desire to be a priest.**
English:en.Tal vez no sabes de dónde viene, pero la:
Maybe you don’t know where it comes from, but the idea of being a priest frequently crosses your mind.  
Sometimes you push it away; other times it strongly attracts you… but it never completely disappears.  
That constant restlessness may be a seed that God has planted in your heart.  
Talk about it! With a priest you admire or someone you know will help you, and tell him what you feel.
English:en.### **2\\. Sientes un gran amor por Cris: ### **2. You feel a great love for Christ, for the Church, and for her teaching.**
English:en.Te entusiasma conocer más sobre la fe, l:
You are excited to learn more about the faith, to read about the saints, doctrine, or the life of the Church.  
You are drawn to the different vocations and charisms, and you are thrilled to see how all serve the same Lord.  
A man called to the priesthood usually feels at home within the Church and enjoys learning “all things Catholic.”
English:en.### **3\\. Otras personas te han dicho q: ### **3. Other people have told you that you would make a good priest.**
English:en.A veces los demás ven algo en ti que tú:
Sometimes others see something in you that you yourself don’t notice.  
When someone says to you, “Have you thought about being a priest? I think you’d be a good one,”  
it may be a way that God speaks to you through them.  
Many priests say that, after hearing that comment repeatedly, they decided to take the step toward the seminary.
English:en.### **4\\. Deseas vivir una vida de orac: ### **4. You desire to live a life of prayer and grow in virtue.**
English:en.El Papa Benedicto XVI decía que lo que s:
Pope Benedict XVI said that what is expected of a priest is that he be an expert in the spiritual life.  
Therefore, one who senses the call strives to attend Mass, pray frequently, go to confession, serve others, and grow interiorly.
English:en.### **5\\. Quieres ayudar a los demás a: ### **5. You want to help others draw closer to Jesus.**
English:en.El sacerdote lleva a Jesús a las persona:
The priest brings Jesus to people and people to Jesus.  
If you feel in your heart the desire to accompany, teach, console, or guide others in their faith,  
it may be that God is calling you to serve as a priest.  
The priestly vocation consists, at its core, in **leading others to heaven.**
English:en.Si has pensado en el sacerdocio, pero no:
If you have thought about the priesthood but don’t see all these qualities in yourself, **don’t worry.**  
No one is born a priest: one becomes a priest with God’s help and the formation the Church offers.
English:en.Habla con tu **Director espiritual** o c:
Speak with your **Spiritual Director** or with a trusted priest.  
They can help you better discern what God is asking of you.  
And remember: true happiness and peace are found **only by following the will of God.**
English:en.Si Él te llama, **su gracia te llevará h: If He calls you, **His grace will carry you to the end.**
English:en.# La Vida Activa: servir al mundo desde: # The Active Life: serving the world through total self-giving to God
English:en.Una de las llamadas más importantes del: One of the most important calls of the **pontificate of John Paul II** has been the **new evangelization**: a commitment that requires enthusiasm, dedication, and unity of all the people of God. Priests, religious, and lay people are summoned to this task, which can only be carried out in **authentic communion**, respecting the diversity of charisms and vocations.
English:en.Dentro de este panorama, los **religioso: Within this panorama, **religious of the Active Life** constitute an **extraordinary potential** for the mission of the Church:
English:en.> “También en nuestros días los religios:
> “Even in our day, religious men and women represent a primary evangelizing and apostolic force on the Latin American continent… The presence of consecrated life is an enormous potential of persons and communities, of charisms and institutions… to permeate cultures, defend human rights, and promote the integral development of persons.”  
> — _Apostolic Letter to the Religious of Latin America, June 29, 1990, n. 3_
English:en.Los religiosos de Vida Activa no solo **: Religious of the Active Life not only **bear witness to the evangelical life**, but **make it present in the midst of society**, bringing the light of Christ where it is most needed.
English:en.La **Vida Activa** se define como la for: The **Active Life** is defined as the form of religious life in which members are dedicated primarily **to works of charity and apostolate**. Unlike the Contemplative Life, centered on prayer and meditation, the Active Life seeks to live the Gospel **through concrete actions**, following the example of Jesus:
English:en.* Atención a enfermos y ancianos: *   Care for the sick and the elderly
English:en.* Obras sociales y asistencia a los ma: *   Social works and assistance to the marginalized
English:en.> “El primer medio de evangelización par:
> “The first means of evangelization for religious is to conform their own life more and more to the person and message of Jesus Christ.”  
> — _Address to the Major Superiors of Europe, November 17, 1983_
English:en.## Características de las religiosas de: ## Characteristics of women religious of the Active Life
English:en.Quienes optan por la Vida Activa poseen: Those who choose the Active Life have distinctive qualities:
English:en.**1\\. Compromiso con el servicio:** \n:
**1\. Commitment to service:**  
They live dedicated to others, especially the most in need.
English:en.**2\\. Versatilidad apostólica:** \nPar:
**2\. Apostolic versatility:**  
They take part in various activities according to their congregation’s apostolate, from education to healthcare.
English:en.**3\\. Vínculo comunitario:** \nLa vida:
**3\. Community bond:**  
Community life strengthens fraternity, shared prayer, and mutual support.
English:en.**4\\. Espiritualidad activa:** \nCombi:
**4\. Active spirituality:**  
They combine prayer, sacramental life, and apostolic action, balancing contemplation and service.
English:en.> “La Iglesia espera de los religiosos y:
> “The Church expects from religious men and women a constant and decisive impulse in the work of the new evangelization, since each is called according to his or her charism to spread throughout the world the good news of Christ.”  
> — _Apostolic Letter to the Religious of Latin America, June 29, 1990, n. 24_
English:en.## Formación de las religiosas de Vida A: ## Formation of women religious of the Active Life
English:en.La formación se realiza en etapas, busca: Formation takes place in stages, seeking to deepen the vocation and discern definitive commitment:
English:en.* **Postulantado:** Introducción a la: *   **Postulancy:** Introduction to community life and apostolate.
English:en.* **Noviciado:** Formación más profund: *   **Novitiate:** Deeper formation in spirituality, charism, and the rules of the congregation.
English:en.* **Profesión temporal:** Votos tempor: *   **Temporary profession:** Renewable temporary vows while discernment continues.
English:en.* **Profesión perpetua:** Votos de por: *   **Perpetual profession:** Lifelong vows, totally consecrating one’s existence to God and to the service of neighbor.
English:en.Esta formación garantiza que las religio: This formation ensures that women religious can **respond to the challenges of the new evangelization** with effectiveness and coherence.
English:en.Las religiosas de Vida Activa viven en *: Women religious of the Active Life live in **open communities**: convents, parishes, or community centers, interacting with society. Their day combines:
English:en.* **Trabajo comunitario:** Tareas de l: *   **Community work:** Household tasks and fraternal life.
English:en.* **Servicio apostólico:** Educación,: *   **Apostolic service:** Education, healthcare, social assistance, and evangelization.
English:en.> “La acción misionera depende de vuestr:
> “Missionary action depends on your missionary formation… no one bears witness more effectively to evangelical values than those who profess consecrated life with total self-giving to God and availability to serve man and society.”  
> — _Redemptoris Missio, nn. 69-70_
English:en.Las religiosas de Vida Activa actúan **d: Women religious of the Active Life work **directly in society**, bringing the Gospel to the world:
English:en.* **Salud:** Atención a enfermos y enf: *   **Health:** Care for the sick and nursing.
English:en.* **Trabajo social:** Apoyo a los pobr: *   **Social work:** Support for the poor and marginalized.
English:en.* **Evangelización:** Catequesis y mis: *   **Evangelization:** Catechesis and local and international missions.
English:en.Su labor es **complementaria a la Vida C: Their work is **complementary to the Contemplative Life**, which sustains the Church through prayer and intercession. Together they enable the Church to fulfill its mission: **to serve and to evangelize integrally**.
English:en.## Vida Contemplativa: Amor, Adoración y: ## Contemplative Life: Love, Adoration, and Hope
English:en.La **vida contemplativa** no es solo ora: The **contemplative life** is not only prayer: it is a call to **love for the sake of Love**. It is to give one’s life to God, to recognize His Love as an absolute value, and to let it fill every moment of our existence. As Saint Elizabeth of the Trinity said, the contemplative soul seeks to be united with God: **to be invaded by Him, to reflect His Life** in every gesture and thought.
English:en.### Orar y Adorar: El corazón de la cont: ### To Pray and Adore: The heart of contemplation
English:en.La contemplación no es solo interceder p: Contemplation is not only interceding for others; it is **total adoration**. In the biblical tradition, holocausts symbolized complete self-offering: what was offered to God was not held back for oneself. Thus, the contemplative life seems “useless” to the world, because it does not seek recognition or reward, but it is a supreme act of love.
English:en.Santa Teresa de Lisieux lo resumía así:: Saint Thérèse of Lisieux summed it up this way: _“In the Heart of my Mother, the Church, I will be LOVE.”_ Contemplatives are that heart: their silent prayer becomes **grace for all**, even if they do not see the fruit of their offering. Everything lived, suffered, and offered becomes a strength that God pours out upon the world.
English:en.En los monasterios, la vida gira alreded: In monasteries, life revolves around prayer: _Ora et labora_ —to pray and to work— defines each day. Meals, work, and rest are organized around the times of prayer.
English:en.Pero no es un aislamiento frío: la comun: But it is not a cold isolation: the community is a living brotherhood. Joy and sorrow are shared, faith is accompanied, and they sustain one another. Monasteries are also a refuge and an open home for those who seek God, whether they are aware of it or not.
English:en.La **esperanza** es el motor de la vida: Hope is the driving force of contemplative life. It is not just wishing, but believing with certainty in the Love of God, even when everything seems dark. It grows in the small acts of prayer, in patience, and in quiet constancy.
English:en.Como explica Hermana Patricia Noya, la e: As Sister Patricia Noya explains, hope does not remain in the cloister: it goes out into the world through prayer, embraces hearts, gathers the small hopes of each person, and offers them to God. Thus, contemplative life becomes a living memory of hope for all.
English:en.Ser contemplativo es **vivir para los de: To be contemplative is to live for others from within silence. Although it may seem “invisible,” its influence is immense: it sustains the Church, accompanies the world, and reminds us that the Love of God is always at work.
English:en.Monjas y monjes, desde su entrega, muest: Nuns and monks, through their self-giving, show that contemplation is not an escape from the world, but a deep and joyful commitment. Their daily prayer is an act of love that transforms reality, even without anyone seeing it.
English:en.En pocas palabras: la vida contemplativa: In a few words: contemplative life is love poured out, constant adoration, and hope that does not die. A call to live the invisible in order to sustain the visible. Because where there is contemplation, there is light, there is grace, and there is hope for all.
English:en.Cada matrimonio es reflejo del amor de D: Every marriage is a reflection of God’s love.
English:en.### **La vocación al matrimonio: entrega: ### The vocation to marriage: total self-giving and holiness in daily life
English:en.La **vocación al matrimonio** es una lla: The vocation to marriage is a call to live an intimate, exclusive, and definitive union between a man and a woman, which reflects the love of God and shares in His plan of love. This union is not just living together, but a lifelong commitment, open to the transmission of life, where the spouses are invited to holiness in the everyday, both in their relationship and in the welcoming and education of their children.
English:en.El matrimonio es, ante todo, **entrega a: Marriage is, above all, self-giving to the other. In total self-giving to one’s spouse, husbands and wives learn to surpass themselves, leave selfishness behind, be at the service of the other, sacrifice themselves, and strive for the other’s happiness. This gift of self is a sign of love for God, which will find its fulfillment in heaven. Every act of self-giving and sacrifice is a reflection of Trinitarian love: as the Father loves the Son and both live in a continual outpouring of the Spirit, spouses learn to live in unity and openness to life.
English:en.### **El matrimonio como signo y partici: ### Marriage as sign and participation in the love of God
English:en.El **matrimonio tiene la misión de custo: Marriage has the mission to safeguard, reveal, and communicate love, being a living reflection and real participation in God’s love for humanity and in Christ’s love for the Church (Amoris Laetitia, 67). This conjugal love is not reduced to affection or feeling: it is total self-giving, made concrete in daily life and a path of sanctification. Each spouse becomes, in a unique way, a sign and instrument of the Lord’s closeness (Amoris Laetitia, 221).
English:en.La **santidad se manifiesta en los peque: Holiness is manifested in small acts of love, generosity, and fidelity. In the words of Pope Francis:
English:en.“Hay muchas formas de ser fieles al esti: “There are many ways of being faithful to the way of life that Jesus proposes for each person… this holiness lived in marriage is a precious reflection of God’s fidelity” (Gaudete et Exsultate, 14).
English:en.El consentimiento matrimonial no es solo: Matrimonial consent is not just a human agreement: it is a covenant raised by Christ to the dignity of a sacrament (CIC 1601). It has a special grace, with three effects:
English:en.3. **Santificación de los cónyuges**, s: 3.  Sanctification of the spouses, as taught by the Second Vatican Council:
English:en.“El Señor se ha dignado sanar este amor,: “The Lord has deigned to heal this love, to perfect it and elevate it with the special gift of grace and charity” (GS 48).
English:en.El **“sí” del matrimonio** significa **e: The “yes” of marriage means mutual, total, and selfless self-giving, in a union expressed in respect, welcome, and mutual help. Marriage excludes selfishness, since each spouse ceases to be the sole owner of themselves and comes to belong to the other as much as to themselves. Personal identity is transformed by the relationship with the other, binding them “until death do you part.” This union of the spouses is the most intimate that exists on earth, and is manifested in the reality of being “one flesh.”
English:en.### **Una vocación en toda la extensión: ### A vocation in every sense of the word
English:en.El matrimonio es **totalmente vocación**: Marriage is wholly a vocation. Once the bond is born, it no longer depends on individual will, but on nature and on God, who has united them. The freedom of each spouse is no longer about choosing to be husband or wife, but about living fully in accord with the truth of what they are: companions on the way, collaborators in holiness, and a living reflection of God’s love in the world.
English:en.Cada matrimonio es un **lugar de santifi: Every marriage is a place of sanctification, a space where human love becomes a testimony of divine love, and each daily self-giving, each act of service and sacrifice, is one more step in participating in Trinitarian life and in building the Kingdom of God on earth.
English:en.Dios te sueña a lo grande. ¿Y tú? ¿Cómo: God dreams big for you. And you? How do you dream yourself? Dare to discover the meaning of your life.
English:en.Contamos con un grupo de discernimiento: We have a broad discernment group for young people and another priestly one for young men. These meetings help clarify one’s vocation and to take concrete steps toward it.
English:en.## Dios no te hizo por casualidad: **te: ## God did not make you by chance: **He dreamed you for something great**.
English:en.Todos caminamos juntos y **lo que vivimo: We all walk together and **what we experience of God is transmitted**.
English:en.No solo rezar: animar: Not only to pray: to encourage
English:en.## **No solo rezar, animar**: ## **Not only to pray, but to encourage**
English:en.A veces parece que las vocaciones —al sa:
Sometimes it seems that vocations—to the priesthood, to marriage, or to consecrated life—**spring from nowhere**, as if God called in secret without anyone else having a part.  
But it isn’t so: **God calls through concrete faces, living communities, people who truly care.**  
That’s why it’s not enough to pray for vocations. **It has to be proposed with one’s life.**
English:en.Rezar por las vocaciones significa **mir:
To pray for vocations means **to look around**: at the young people in your parish, your friends, those who serve with joy, those who listen with patience, those who live the faith with integrity.  
When you see someone like that, **don’t keep quiet**. It may be time to raise, with simplicity and discretion:
English:en.> “¿Has pensado a qué te llama Dios?”: » “Have you thought about what God is calling you to?”
English:en.A veces una pregunta como esa, lanzada p: Sometimes a question like that, posed by a **priest**, a **catechist**, or a **believing friend**, awakens vocational discernment that may have been sidelined by oneself.
English:en.El mundo necesita **buenos esposos**, **: The world needs **good spouses**, **good priests**, **good consecrated women**. And that begins when each person is a good example of his or her vocation and strives to show the joy of self-giving.
English:en.## ¡Discierne tu vocación! Dios no te hi: ## Discern your vocation! God did not make you by chance: **He dreamed you for something great**.
English:en.2. Etapa Propedeutica: afianzar la voca: 2. Propedeutic Stage: strengthen the vocation for one year, growing in prayer and community life.
English:en.3. Etapa Discipular: crecer en la confi: 3. Discipular Stage: grow in configuration with Christ while completing **two years of Philosophy**.
English:en.4. Etapa Configuradora: **asimilar el c: 4. Configurative Stage: **assimilate the heart of Christ the Shepherd and Servant** by completing **three years of Theology**.
English:en.## 🙏 Jornada Mundial de Oración por las: ## 🙏 World Day of Prayer for Vocations
English:en.En comunión con toda la Iglesia, celebra: In communion with the whole Church, we celebrate a day especially dedicated to **praying for vocations**. A moment to return to what is essential: **listening to the voice of God** who continues calling today, personally, each one.
English:en.💡 La vocación no es solo para unos pocos:
💡 Vocation is not only for a few:  
it is a concrete, real, and unique call that God addresses to each person. That is why this day is an invitation to:
English:en.- 🙏 **Rezar juntos**, como Iglesia, por:
- 🙏 **Pray together**, as Church, for all vocations
- ❤️ **Pray for young people**, so that they may discover their path
- 👂 **Dispose ourselves interiorly** to listen to Jesus Christ
- 🔥 **Enjoy a concert and celebrate,** God continues calling
English:en.Queremos vivir este día **de forma concr: We want to live this day **in a concrete and communal way**, gathering to share faith, joy, and the sincere search for the will of God.
English:en.📍 **Lugar:** Seminario de Pamplona \n🏟:
📍 **Place:** Seminary of Pamplona  
🏟 **Space:** Sacred Heart Pavilion
English:en.- 🕗 **20:00 – Oración** \nUn tiempo fue:
- 🕗 **8:00 PM – Prayer**  
A strong time to place our life before the Lord and listen to His call.
- 🍽 **9:00 PM – Shared dinner**  
A moment of encounter, friendship, and community.
- 🎶 **10:00 PM – Concert**  
We joyfully celebrate that **each vocation is a gift for everyone**.
English:en.- Jóvenes de **16 a 35 años**\n- De toda:
- Young people **16 to 35 years old**
- From all **Navarre**
English:en.No hace falta tenerlo todo claro. \nSol:
You don’t need to have everything clear.  
You only need to be **willing**.
English:en.En esta jornada queremos recordar algo f: On this day we want to remember something fundamental:
English:en.👉 **Dios sigue llamando hoy** \n👉 **Cad:
👉 **God continues calling today**  
👉 **Each vocation builds up the Church**  
👉 **We are all responsible for praying for everyone**
English:en.Porque cuando uno descubre su vocación…:
Because when someone discovers his or her vocation…  
✨ **the whole Church rejoices** ✨
English:en.Para participar, inscríbete en el formul:
To participate, register using the form:  
👉 [https://forms.gle/Tj2f6GeWdq3sgw3R7](https://forms.gle/Tj2f6GeWdq3sgw3R7)
English:en.🙏 *Contamos contigo. Reza, ven, particip:
🙏 *We’re counting on you. Pray, come, take part… and let yourself be surprised by what God can do.*  
🔥 **We all pray for everyone!**
English:en.## ¡Discierne tu vocación! Dios no te hi: ## Discern your vocation! God did not make you by chance: **He dreamed you for something great**

Español:es.### **¡Lleva tu parroquia al siguiente n:
### **¡Lleva tu parroquia al siguiente nivel digital!**

Hoy en día, tener una presencia online no es un lujo, ¡es una necesidad! Y no te preocupes, no tienes que ser un experto en tecnología para hacer que tu parroquia brille en la web. Nosotros nos encargamos de la parte técnica para que tú puedas concentrarte en lo que realmente importa: la comunidad.

**¿Por qué tener una web para tu parroquia?**  
Una página web no solo es una tarjeta de presentación digital, ¡es un centro de conexión para tus feligreses! Aquí tienes algunas razones por las que es indispensable:

*   **Conéctate con todos**: Los feligreses siempre tendrán acceso a la información más actualizada, desde horarios de misas hasta eventos especiales.
    
*   **Notificaciones al instante**: ¡Nada de olvidos! Las notificaciones automáticas te permiten enviar recordatorios de eventos, misas y actividades directamente a los móviles de la comunidad.
    
*   **Accesibilidad desde cualquier lugar**: La web funciona como una app en los teléfonos, ¡y va contigo a todas partes!
    

### **Tu contenido, tu estilo, tu comunidad**

Nosotros te damos la libertad de elegir qué mostrar en tu web, y lo hacemos fácil y rápido. Además, personalizamos todo para que tu página tenga el estilo único de tu parroquia.

### **Todo lo que necesitas, sin complicaciones**

El objetivo es que puedas gestionar todo tú mismo de forma sencilla. Nada de complicados paneles de control ni tecnología que te haga sentir como si estuvieras programando un cohete. Nuestra plataforma es fácil, intuitiva, ¡y con la garantía de que siempre te ayudamos en lo que necesites!

Así que, si quieres que tu parroquia esté conectada, visible y activa en el mundo digital, ¡estamos aquí para ayudarte! Da el paso sin miedo.
Español:es.Necestimaos un nuevo tejado en la parroq: Necesitamos un nuevo tejado en la parroquia...
Español:es.¿Cómo puedo solicitar el sacramento del: ¿Cómo puedo solicitar el sacramento del Bautismo para mi hijo/a?
Español:es.Para solicitar el Bautismo, debes ponert: Para solicitar el Bautismo, debes ponerte en contacto con la parroquia al menos dos semanas antes de la fecha deseada. Los padres y padrinos deben participar en una breve preparación, que se puede hacer de forma presencial o en línea. ¡Es un momento hermoso, así que asegúrate de planificar con antelación!
Español:es.¿Puedo añadir preguntas y respuestas a m: ¿Puedo añadir preguntas y respuestas a mi web?
Español:es.Sí, claro. Añadir una sección de **FAQ**:
Sí, claro. Añadir una sección de **Preguntas Frecuentes** (FAQ) a la web de tu parroquia es una excelente manera de hacerla más accesible y ayudar a los feligreses con dudas comunes. Aquí te dejo algunos consejos para hacerlo de manera eficaz:

1.  **Organiza por categorías**: Si tienes varias áreas de interés (sacramentos, actividades, voluntariado, etc.), agrúpalas para que sea más fácil encontrar la información.
    
2.  **Actualiza regularmente**: Asegúrate de revisar y actualizar las respuestas para reflejar cambios o nuevos procedimientos.
    
3.  **Hazlo accesible**: Coloca un enlace visible en el menú o en la página de inicio para que los visitantes puedan acceder fácilmente.
    
4.  **Usa un diseño limpio**: No satures la página con demasiada información. Lo ideal es mantener cada respuesta breve y directa.
Español:es.Con una imagen de fondo. Y llamadas a la: Con una imagen de fondo. Y llamadas a la acción
Español:es.San Frutos nació en el 642 de una noble: San Frutos nació en el 642 de una noble familia segoviana
Español:es.Jesús lo miró con amor y le dijo ― [...]: Jesús lo miró con amor y le dijo ― [...] ve, vende todo lo que tienes y dalo a los pobres [...] luego ven y sígueme. (Mc 10, 21)
Español:es.San Frutos y sus hermanos Valentín y Eng: San Frutos y sus hermanos Valentín y Engracia, decidieron seguir la llamada de Dios y dejaron todo su dinero a los pobres
Español:es.San Frutos y sus hermanos fueron acogido: San Frutos y sus hermanos fueron acogidos en la comunidad eremítica del Duratón
Español:es.Dedicaban su tiempo a la oración: Dedicaban su tiempo a la oración
Español:es.Y el trabajo en la huerta: Y el trabajo en la huerta
Español:es.En el año 711 los musulmanes invaden la: En el año 711 los musulmanes invaden la península
Español:es.San Frutos arriesgó valientemente su vid: San Frutos arriesgó valientemente su vida para defender la Fe y a sus hermanos
Español:es.Muriendo poco después (c. 715) en compañ: Muriendo poco después (c. 715) en compañía de sus hermanos
Español:es.Poco después los soldados musulmanes irr: Poco después los soldados musulmanes irrumpen en el monasterio de Caballar, decapitando a Valentín y Engracia
Español:es.Tras la reconquista, el obispo inaugura: Tras la reconquista, el obispo inaugura un priorato benedictino en 1200
Español:es.Tiempo de abandono y hundimiento del pri: Tiempo de abandono y hundimiento del priorato
Español:es.Hoy día, se está recuperando la tradició: Hoy día, se está recuperando la tradición orante de este lugar
Español:es.### **¡Lleva tu parroquia al siguiente n: ### **¡Lleva tu parroquia al siguiente nivel digital!**
Español:es.Hoy en día, tener una presencia online n: Hoy en día, tener una presencia online no es un lujo, ¡es una necesidad! Y no te preocupes, no tienes que ser un experto en tecnología para hacer que tu parroquia brille en la web. Nosotros nos encargamos de la parte técnica para que tú puedas concentrarte en lo que realmente importa: la comunidad.
Español:es.**¿Por qué tener una web para tu parroqu:
**¿Por qué tener una web para tu parroquia?**  
Una página web no solo es una tarjeta de presentación digital, ¡es un centro de conexión para tus feligreses! Aquí tienes algunas razones por las que es indispensable:
Español:es.* **Conéctate con todos**: Los feligre: *   **Conéctate con todos**: Los feligreses siempre tendrán acceso a la información más actualizada, desde horarios de misas hasta eventos especiales.
Español:es.* **Notificaciones al instante**: ¡Nad: *   **Notificaciones al instante**: ¡Nada de olvidos! Las notificaciones automáticas te permiten enviar recordatorios de eventos, misas y actividades directamente a los móviles de la comunidad.
Español:es.* **Accesibilidad desde cualquier luga: *   **Accesibilidad desde cualquier lugar**: La web funciona como una app en los teléfonos, ¡y va contigo a todas partes!
Español:es.Nosotros te damos la libertad de elegir: Te damos la libertad de elegir qué mostrar en tu web, y lo hacemos fácil y rápido. Además, personalizamos todo para que tu página tenga el estilo único de tu parroquia.
Español:es.El objetivo es que puedas gestionar todo: El objetivo es que puedas gestionar todo tú mismo de forma sencilla. Nada de paneles de control complicados ni tecnología que te haga sentir como si estuvieras programando un cohete. Nuestra plataforma es fácil, intuitiva, ¡y con la garantía de que siempre te ayudamos en lo que necesites!
Español:es.Así que, si quieres que tu parroquia est: Así que, si quieres que tu parroquia esté conectada, visible y activa en el mundo digital, ¡estamos aquí para ayudarte! Da el paso sin miedo.
Español:es.Sí, claro. Añadir una sección de **FAQ**: Sí, claro. Añadir una sección de **FAQ** (preguntas frecuentes) a la web de tu parroquia es una excelente manera de hacerla más accesible y ayudar a los feligreses con dudas comunes. Aquí te dejo algunos consejos para hacerlo de manera eficaz:
Español:es.1. **Organiza por categorías**: Si tien: 1.  **Organiza por categorías**: Si tienes varias áreas de interés (sacramentos, actividades, voluntariado, etc.), agrúpalas para que sea más fácil encontrar la información.
Español:es.2. **Actualiza regularmente**: Asegúrat: 2.  **Actualiza regularmente**: Asegúrate de revisar y actualizar las respuestas para reflejar cambios o nuevos procedimientos.
Español:es.3. **Hazlo accesible**: Coloca un enlac: 3.  **Hazlo accesible**: Coloca un enlace visible en el menú o en la página de inicio para que los visitantes puedan acceder fácilmente.
Español:es.4. **Usa un diseño limpio**: No satures: 4.  **Usa un diseño limpio**: No satures la página con demasiada información. Lo ideal es mantener cada respuesta breve y directa.
Español:es.**¿Cómo conocer el plan de Dios para tu:
**¿Cómo conocer el plan de Dios para tu vida?**  
¡Sigue estas recomendaciones y Dios te ayudará a comprender a qué te está llamando en tu vida!
Español:es.1. **Oración** \n La oración es una:
1.  **Oración**  
    La oración es una conversación con Dios, no solo recitar oraciones, sino hablar con el Señor desde el corazón. Todos los días lee la Biblia y habla con Jesús como si fuera tu mejor amigo. Pídele que te revele Su plan: “Jesús, quiero querer lo que Tú quieres. Aquí estoy, Señor; vengo a hacer Tu voluntad.” Confía plenamente en que Dios tiene un plan para ti.
Español:es.2. **Sacramentos** \n El alma es la:
2.  **Sacramentos**  
    El alma es la ventana por la que podemos ver a Dios y escuchar Su voz. Si la ventana está sucia por el pecado, debe limpiarse mediante la Confesión para poder estar en unión con Dios. Cuando recibas la Comunión, pídele a Jesús que te muestre tu vocación.
Español:es.3. **Buena información** \n Es impo:
3.  **Buena información**  
    Es imposible descubrir tu vocación sin buena información. ¿Qué significa tener un matrimonio cristiano y santo? ¿Qué hacen los sacerdotes? ¿Cómo es la vida de una hermana o un hermano religioso? Busca información confiable y luego lleva lo aprendido de vuelta a la oración.
Español:es.4. **La Santísima Virgen** \n María:
4.  **La Santísima Virgen**  
    María es el ejemplo máximo de apertura a la voluntad de Dios. ¿Estás abierto a la voluntad de Dios para ti? Reza tres Avemarías cada día, específicamente para conocer tu vocación y tener el valor de seguirla.
Español:es.5. **Ir de retiro** \n ¡Los milagro:
5.  **Ir de retiro**  
    ¡Los milagros ocurren en los retiros! Una de las maneras más seguras de descubrir el plan de Dios para ti es entrar en el silencio y la oración durante un tiempo prolongado. Pide a personas de confianza que te ayuden a encontrar un buen retiro.
Español:es.6. **Pide consejo** \n No trates de:
6.  **Pide consejo**  
    No trates de discernir tu vocación solo, sin la ayuda de la Iglesia. Habla con una hermana, un sacerdote o un diácono en quien confíes.
Español:es.Cuando llegue el momento, ¡debes actuar!: Cuando llegue el momento, ¡debes actuar! Muévete en la dirección que te indique tu corazón, con la confianza de que Dios nunca te enviará a un lugar donde Su gracia no pueda sostenerte.
Español:es.## **Cada vocación es un misterio: cada: ## **Cada vocación es un misterio: cada hombre pasa por estas etapas de manera diferente.**
Español:es.Algunos solo pasarán por las etapas para: Algunos solo pasarán por las etapas para descubrir el sentido de su vida laical u otros para encontrar su vocación sacerdotal.
Español:es.Si estás en la etapa 1 has experimentado: Si estás en la etapa 1 has experimentado recientemente una llamada a entregarte al Señor. Esta etapa inicial de discernimiento suele ocurrir durante la infancia, quizás en la Misa, por ejemplo de religiosos, viendo cristianos que aman radicalmente a Dios, gracias a catequistas comprometidos, con tus padres, en una clase de escuela católica o cuando un sacerdote te invita a considerar el sacerdocio.
Español:es.Esta atracción inicial y misteriosa por: Esta atracción inicial y misteriosa por dárselo todo a Dios es misteriosa. Convertirse en sacerdote, entregarse a la vida religiosa, ser sal y luz en medio de todos los hombres… Es aún más misteriosa porque no se tiene casi ninguna información sobre la vocación o el discernimiento.
Español:es.Las emociones principales son emoción y: Las emociones principales son emoción y asombro: “¿Realmente podría Dios llamarme a darme enteramente?”
Español:es.Piensas en ello de vez en cuando, pero n: Piensas en ello de vez en cuando, pero no todos los días. La idea aparece en la Misa, al rezar por la noche o cuando alguien lo menciona. Pero piensas: “Seguro que no es para mí”, “No me hace falta nada más” “Ya voy a misa y rezo algo”... “Ser laico no es una vocación” o “Me casaré como todos”
Español:es.Te das cuenta de que esa llamada no desa: Te das cuenta de que esa llamada no desaparece, aunque lo intentes. Cedes y dejas de evitarla. Empiezas a orar más, a crecer en la fe y a buscar ayuda de un director espiritual. Asimismo, encuentras a Cristo en tu hermano y en los necesitados te abres a la entrega concreta a los demás. Comienzas a luchar la batalla espiritual de entregarse a la voluntad de Dios; algunos días ganas la batalla y otros días la pierdes. En esta etapa descubres una paz distinta, una que nace al profundizar en tu vida interior y al llevar a Cristo a cada aspecto de tu vida. Aquí aprendes a vencer miedos: el temor de perderte cosas, de no ser suficiente o de que te tachen de “santo”.
Español:es.Igual no encuentras todavía paz y confir: Igual no encuentras todavía paz y confirmación, descubres que tus miedos son al sacerdocio o a la vida religiosa: celibato, soledad, hablar en público, ser un buen sacerdote… los afrontas y sientes la alegría de servir a Dios y a los demás. Tu deseo de servir a los demás crece y experimentas felicidad mientras sirves.
Español:es.Vas avanzando hacia la entrega a la volu: Vas avanzando hacia la entrega a la voluntad de Dios. Tras rezar, informarte y participar en retiros, sientes paz y confirmas la llamada de Dios a dejarlo todo.
Español:es.Tu director espiritual te dice que tiene: Tu director espiritual te dice que tiene las señales y cualificaciones. El elemento esencial es comprender que el discernimiento no puede avanzar más fuera del seminario o del noviciado; estás simplemente dando vueltas en círculos.
Español:es.Entras al seminario o al noviciado y viv: Entras al seminario o al noviciado y vives rodeado de otros que también están discerniendo y tu vida gira en torno a la oración diaria, la Misa y el estudio serio de la fe.
Español:es.Habías descubierto la llamada de Dios a: Habías descubierto la llamada de Dios a vivir como laico y empiezas a fijarte en quién podría ser tu pareja confiando en Dios. Empezáis a salir y vivir el noviazgo como discernimiento: conocimiento mutuo prudente y
Español:es.Es el momento de consolidar tu camino es: Es el momento de consolidar tu camino espiritual y vocacional.
Español:es.Llevas tiempo en el seminario y has crec: Llevas tiempo en el seminario y has crecido mucho en fe, oración y conocimiento. Conoces a Jesús de manera personal y estás listo para enseñar y servir. Ahora el trabajo duro es diario, pero sientes que seguramente serás sacerdote y sabes que puedes cumplir con esa responsabilidad.
Español:es.Si estás a punto de casarte, también pue: Si estás a punto de casarte, también puedes experimentar esta paz interior que nace de vivir tu vocación con entrega. Conoces a Jesús personalmente y estás listo para amar, enseñar y servir dentro de tu familia. El trabajo diario es constante: construir un hogar sólido, acompañar a tu pareja, educar a los hijos y vivir la fe juntos. Sientes que Dios te ha llamado a esta misión y sabes que, con su ayuda, puedes ordenar toda la vida social desde dentro y transformar el mundo a través del amor y la entrega de tu familia.
Español:es.## Los votos religiosos: pobreza, castid: ## Los votos religiosos: pobreza, castidad y obediencia, camino de amor total
Español:es.**Por amor a Cristo, muchos hombres y mu: **Por amor a Cristo, muchos hombres y mujeres en la Iglesia lo dejan todo.** No por desprecio del mundo, sino por amor al Reino que viene. En un tiempo marcado por la búsqueda del placer, la riqueza y la autonomía personal, los votos religiosos son una **luz profética** que muestra otro modo de vivir: el de **Jesús pobre, casto y obediente**.
Español:es.Un _voto_ es una **promesa hecha a Dios*: Un _voto_ es una **promesa hecha a Dios**. No es un simple propósito humano, sino un compromiso sagrado por el cual una persona se entrega al Señor con todo su ser. El **Código de Derecho Canónico** lo define como una “promesa libre y deliberada hecha a Dios de un bien posible y mejor” (cf. c. 1191).
Español:es.Entre todos los votos, destacan los **vo: Entre todos los votos, destacan los **votos religiosos**, mediante los cuales los miembros de los institutos de vida consagrada se comprometen solemnemente a vivir los _consejos evangélicos_: **pobreza, castidad y obediencia**. Estos no son simples renuncias, sino **formas de amar más y mejor**, reproduciendo en la propia vida el estilo mismo de Jesucristo.
Español:es.> “Por medio de los votos de pobreza, ca:
> “Por medio de los votos de pobreza, castidad y obediencia, ustedes pueden ser portadores de luz para las mujeres y los hombres de nuestro tiempo.”  
> — _Papa Francisco, Jornada Mundial de la Vida Consagrada_
Español:es.### ✨ El voto de castidad: amar con cora: ### ✨ El voto de castidad: amar con corazón indiviso
